payload.title: Improve tmux status-line menu labels and content density
payload.description: Polish + content expansion: (1) Menu title shows `Endless [E-NNN]` via `#()` substitution so the current task ID is visible at the top of the popup. (2) Rename menu item `Detail` → `Task Details`. (3) Center the prefix+e popup with `display-menu -x C -y C` (currently anchors to mouse/status position). (4) Extend status-line printer output to include type, phase, and tier between project and status, final order: `E-NNN · project · type · phase · tier · status`. Requires extending `ActiveTaskInfo` struct in `internal/monitor/tmux_lookup.go` and the SQL query that populates it, plus updating `format()` in `cmd/endless-tmux/status_line.go`.

payload.title: Investigate per-row mouse events for status-format[1] (bind right-click directly to row 1)
payload.description: Today the right-click menu is anchored to row-0 `MouseDown3StatusRight` because E-1236 assumed tmux did not provide per-region mouse events scoped to `status-format[N>0]`. Investigate whether tmux 3.x supports `MouseDown3Status1`-style events for additional status rows, plus `#[range=user|<name>]` and `#[push-default]`/`#[pop-default]` workarounds for clickable regions inside the format string. Research prompt being run via Claude Web; this task captures the implementation once research returns.

fields.outcome:
Fixed: `internal/monitor/tmux_lookup.go` fallback narrowed from session-scoped (`tmux list-panes -s -t`) to window-scoped (`tmux list-panes -t`). Function renamed `listPanesInSameSession` → `listPanesInSameWindow`.

Synthetic test in dev tmux (multiple windows each with their own Claude session):
- pane in window 5 → E-1245
- pane in window 4 → E-1203
- pane in window 1 → E-1135

Different windows now resolve to different tasks as expected.

Post-merge action: `just install` then `endless tmux apply` (same dance as E-1236) so /usr/local/bin/endless-tmux is updated and the running tmux invokes the fixed binary.

Shipped all four items:

1. Menu title — now `Endless [E-NNN]` resolved at click time via the new `endless-tmux show-menu` subcommand. Falls back to plain `Endless` when no active task is found in the focused window.

2. `Detail` → `Task Details` label rename.

3. Prefix+e popup centered with `-x C -y C` on the focused pane. Right-click variants (MouseDown3StatusRight / M-MouseDown3StatusRight) still anchor to the mouse position (`-x M -y M`) so the menu appears at the click point.

4. Status-line printer output extended to: `[E-NNN] · project · type · phase · tier · status`. Empty/nil fields are skipped so the row stays compact (tier defaults to NULL or 0 and is omitted for tasks without one). `ActiveTaskInfo` struct gained Type, Phase, Tier (the last as *int64 for SQL NULL handling); SELECT extended accordingly.

Architectural improvement: menu construction is now in Go (`cmd/endless-tmux/show_menu.go`), so future menu changes don't require re-running apply — only the binary needs to be rebuilt. Bindings are simple `run-shell '<bin> show-menu --pane=#{pane_id} --position=center|mouse'` calls.

Post-merge action: same as E-1236 / E-1245 — `just install` + `endless tmux apply` from main to refresh the running tmux server.

Deliverable: a single markdown research doc with prioritized next-slice recommendations.

# Spawn prompt for E-987 / E-1019 — verbs.json land-conflict design

You're working on E-987 (research: resolve tension between auto-appends and clean-main precondition), with E-1019 (recovery helper for auto-file rebase conflicts during land) and E-968 (parent epic) as direct context. Start by reading those three task records and any plan/text attached to them.

## Why this is being spawned now

On 2026-05-11 Mike landed E-1240 ("add `completed` status gated by verbs.json `completable` flag"). The land hit a `git rebase` conflict on `.endless/verbs.json`. The conflict was resolved manually and the branch landed, so this is no longer blocking. But it surfaced a design hole that E-1019's current v1 plan does not handle correctly.

The hole: `endless worktree land`'s rebase-failure error message currently says

```
Recover: from the worktree, run
  git checkout main -- .endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl .endless/plans/snapshots/* .endless/verbs.json
then retry land.
```

That hint is **safe** when the worktree-side change to one of those files was ambient auto-record dirt (the worktree wasn't supposed to be writing it, and discarding the worktree side preserves intent). It is **unsafe** when the worktree-side change is intentional feature content that happens to touch one of those auto-managed files.

E-1240 is the first concrete unsafe instance: it added a new optional `completable` schema field to `.endless/verbs.json` plus 7 new verb entries — all intentional, all part of the feature. Blindly running the recovery hint would have silently destroyed that work.

The concrete conflict on E-1240's land:

```
Auto-merging .endless/verbs.json
CONFLICT (content): Merge conflict in .endless/verbs.json
Auto-merging internal/web/assets/css/output.css
Auto-merging src/endless/cli.py
```

Main had appended one verb (`bind`) via auto-record. The E-1240 branch had its own additions in the same trailing region of the array. Both sides needed preserving — a structural merge, not a "discard worktree side" reset.

## Your job (design, not implementation)

Enter plan mode and propose how `endless worktree land` should differentiate these two cases and what it should do in each. Cover:

1. **Detection.** How do we distinguish "auto-dirt conflict on an auto-managed file" from "feature-content conflict on an auto-managed file"? Candidate signals to evaluate (not necessarily endorse):
   - Commit metadata — is this commit on the worktree branch authored by the auto-record path (commit message starts with "Endless: auto-record session activity")?
   - Content-shape — did the worktree add NEW top-level keys / NEW array entries by `value` / new schema fields, vs. only mutate values of pre-existing entries?
   - Explicit feature-intent marker on the commit (trailer / branch convention)
   - Whether the file is touched outside the conflict-causing commit on the worktree branch

2. **Action.** What `endless worktree land` should do when the unsafe case is detected. Refuse with a clear message? Drop into an interactive structural-merge helper specialized per file type (verbs.json has known schema)? Auto-merge for cases the dedup logic already handles (E-1141)? Document only? Pick a default and justify.

3. **Scope home.** Whether this work belongs as expansion to E-1019, a sibling task under E-968, or splits across both. Account for E-987's framing — E-987 is scoped to event logs (append-only); verbs.json is structured-with-schema, so the right design may diverge from event-log handling.

4. **verbs.json-specific angle.** The existing dedup helper (`worktree_cmd._dedup_worktree_verbs_against_main` from E-1141/E-1138) already performs a value-level merge for the auto-commit path. Can / should that logic be reused for the rebase-conflict path? Where do its assumptions break (e.g., when the worktree intentionally modifies the SCHEMA of existing entries, like adding `completable: true`)?

## Constraints

- Design only. Do **not** modify code in this spawn.
- Bring Mike in for the design discussion via plan mode before writing the plan to text.
- Save the resulting plan via `endless task update <id> --text <path>` once the target task is identified or filed.
- If the design splits into multiple tasks, file follow-ups under E-968 and link them with the appropriate `relates_to` / `blocks` relations.
- Memory: respect "When in doubt, ASK" and "Discuss before updating" — don't unilaterally lock decisions on E-987's scope.

## Reference material in the repo

- `.endless/plans/E-968.md` (parent epic plan, if present)
- E-987 `--text` field (existing options analysis)
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` — search for `_dedup_worktree_verbs_against_main` and surrounding land logic
- The current rebase-failure error message — search for "Recover:" in `worktree_cmd.py`
- E-1240's landed commit (now on main) — `git log -1 --stat <commit>` for a concrete example of feature additions to verbs.json

fields.text:
# E-1248: Rename `quick-start` → `guide`, restructure as multi-section guide, revise content

## Context

`endless quick-start` echoes a markdown file that onboards a Claude Code session to working with Endless. Three problems:

1. **Verb is wrong.** `quick-start` implies a brief intro, but the doc is the canonical session reference. `guide` is honest about what comes out, no semantic mismatch with CLI conventions (unlike `info`, which conventionally means runtime/system state).
2. **Doc is stale.** Written 2026-04-15; today is 2026-05-11. ~50+ session-facing tasks have shipped since — `task spawn`/`task prompt`, the per-task worktree model (E-1168), `task start` → `task claim` rename (E-1232), commit-to-main policy (E-1199/1200), tmux integration (E-1236), decisions as first-class items (E-907/981), and more. The date-stamped filename implies a snapshot when the file is meant to be evergreen.
3. **Single dump doesn't scale.** As coverage grows, dumping the whole guide on every cold session is wasteful. A section-addressable form (`endless guide <section>`) lets agents and humans pull just what they need.

Outcome: the command becomes `endless guide [<section>]`, content lives in `docs/guide/index.md` (top-level index) plus `docs/guide/<section>.md` files, and the content reflects what a session needs to know on 2026-05-11.

**Author voice rule (critical).** Endless is used by many developers — not just one. The current guide names "Mike" repeatedly ("If Mike gives you a task ID…", "only Mike does that"). That is **wrong** for a doc that ships with the tool: when another developer's Claude session reads it, "Mike" is a confusing stranger. The rewrite uses generic terms throughout — *the user*, *your user*, *they* — wherever the current guide says "Mike". This applies to every section, not just the decisions section.

## Command surface change

`endless guide` (no arg) → prints `docs/guide/index.md` (the index — short overview + list of sections, each line ≈ `<section>: <one-line summary>` so the agent can pick).

`endless guide <section>` → prints `docs/guide/<section>.md` (raises a clean error listing valid sections if unknown).

`endless guide --list` → prints just the available section slugs (cheap for an agent to check; excludes `index`).

Implementation: `src/endless/cli.py` — `@main.command("guide")` taking optional positional `section` and `--list` flag. Reads from `docs/guide/<section>.md` when given, else `docs/guide/index.md`. Unknown section: enumerate `docs/guide/*.md` (minus `index.md`) and raise `ClickException` with the list.

## Section layout

`docs/guide/index.md` (index — keep ≤ 80 lines):
- 2-paragraph "what Endless is + current dev status"
- The happy-path one-pager (the most common case: claim → work → verify)
- Section index — one line per section pointing readers at `endless guide <section>`

`docs/guide/` files:

| Section            | Slug         | Notes |
|--------------------|--------------|-------|
| Task lifecycle     | `lifecycle`  | statuses, phases, blocking semantics |
| Task fields        | `fields`     | title/description/text/prompt/analysis/notes/outcome — what each is, what loads it |
| Task commands      | `tasks`      | full CRUD reference: add/update/move/remove/search/release/decline/replace/link/unlink/relations/clear/import/detail. Note `--llm` flag everywhere. |
| Spawn another session | `spawn`   | `task spawn` + `task prompt` + `task update --prompt` workflow |
| Worktrees          | `worktree`   | auto-created on claim; land/drop lifecycle; commit-to-main policy folds in here |
| Decisions          | `decisions`  | first-class artifact; STRONG instructions (see below); inline `--decision` |
| Channels           | `channels`   | inter-session messaging — refresh of current text |
| Snapshots          | `snapshots`  | `endless snapshots list/show` — plan-file mirrors from PostToolUse hook |
| Verbs              | `verbs`      | `endless verb list/add/remove`; verbs validate task title prefixes |
| Project setup      | `projects`   | `endless register`, `endless list`, `endless status`; for unregistered repos |
| SQL queries        | `sql`        | read-only queries against the DB (mention `--write` exists for admin) |
| Tmux integration   | `tmux`       | one short paragraph: "exists, evolving fast, see `endless tmux --help`" — don't document the UI |
| File layout        | `layout`     | where the DB/ledger/config/binaries live (current "Where Things Live" content, refreshed) |
| Common patterns    | `patterns`   | starting work, recording revisits, what's in progress — current section refreshed |

## Files to modify

1. **`src/endless/cli.py:247-254`** — replace `quick-start` command with `guide` accepting optional `section` positional and `--list` flag. Read from `docs/guide/index.md` or `docs/guide/<section>.md`.
2. **`docs/guide-2026-04-15-using-endless-in-sessions.md`** → delete after content migrates (`git rm`).
3. **Create `docs/guide/index.md`** — the new index file (≤ 80 lines).
4. **Create `docs/guide/<slug>.md`** — one file per section in the table above.
5. **`docs/TASK_RECORDING_PROMPT.md:57`** — update path reference to `docs/guide/index.md`.
6. **`demo/10-channels.md:11,19`** — `endless quick-start` → `endless guide`.
7. **`demo/.claude/settings.local.json:4`** — `Bash(endless quick-start:*)` → `Bash(endless guide:*)`.
8. **Historical plan files** under `.endless/plans/` and `deploy/machine/demo/prompt-beads-comparison.md` — **leave alone**, they're historical.

## Reconsidered scope (per Mike's feedback)

**Added to scope:**
- **`endless verb` CRUD** — sessions do add new verbs occasionally when proposing titles that fail validation. Document `verb list/add/remove`. Brief.
- **`endless sql`** — useful for sessions doing read-only investigation (e.g. "how many tasks in `verify`?"). Document the read-only default and that `--write` exists (without encouraging it). Note even if some flags aren't implemented yet, document what is shipping.
- **`endless register`** — sessions occasionally encounter unregistered repos. Brief mention with `endless list`/`endless status`.

**Still out:**
- `endless setup`, `discover`, `scan`, `rename`, `unregister`, `purge`, `suggestions`, `phrase`, `db migrate` — admin/maintenance, not session work.
- E-894 Go-query refactor mechanics.

## Field distinctions (revised — `fields` section content)

Cover all task body fields:
- `title` — one-liner; verb-first per E-723/947.
- `description` — brief blurb (<200 words), shown by default in list/show (E-1058).
- `text` — full implementation plan. Set with `--text <file>`. Shown with `task show --text`.
- `prompt` — task-specific LLM prompt (what a spawned session sees). Set with `--prompt <file>`. Shown with `task show --prompt` or printed raw with `task prompt <id>`.
- `analysis` — supporting content developed via research/exploration that is not a proper plan. Lifecycle and rules per E-1073.
- `notes` — freeform content that doesn't fit elsewhere. Use sparingly.
- `outcome` — required at terminal statuses (`confirm`, `assume`) per E-1021/1022/1175. For `decline` use `--reason`.

Verify field names exist before publishing by reading `endless task add --help`, `endless task update --help`, and the schema. Don't trust the doc draft against memory — read the live CLI.

## Decisions section (revised — STRONG instructions)

Lead the `decisions` section with explicit guidance to sessions (generic — no proper names):

> **Always document decisions where applicable.** When you and your user resolve a non-obvious question — choosing between approaches, scoping in/out, picking conventions — file it. Use `endless task add --type decision` or attach inline via `--decision <text>` on the originating task.
>
> **Verify with the user before stating a decision as binding.** Especially: do not record a *prohibition* when the user only expressed a *preference*. "Ideally we'd …" / "usually we …" / "I'd prefer …" are soft signals — they describe a preference, not a rule. Confirm the rule and its scope before writing a decision. When in doubt, ask.
>
> Inline rationale via `task add ... --decision "<rationale>"` (E-981) is preferred over manually creating a separate decision task — it pairs the decision with the task that prompted it via a `documents` link.

Then list the mechanics: `endless decision list`, `endless decision link`, `endless task add --type decision`, the `--decision` flag.

## Tmux section (revised — brief mention only)

One short section. Verbatim spirit:

> Endless ships a tmux integration that puts the active task ID / project / status on a second status row. Apply with `endless tmux apply`. The integration is being **actively expanded right now** — menus, hotkeys, and layout are likely to change soon. Don't memorize the UI. Run `endless tmux --help` for the currently shipping verbs.

Don't document hotkeys, menu layout, or right-click behavior.

## Verification

After editing:

1. **Build & install**: `just install` from main checkout.
2. **Command rename**:
   - `endless --help` lists `guide`, not `quick-start`.
   - `endless guide` echoes `docs/guide/index.md`.
   - `endless guide spawn` echoes `docs/guide/spawn.md`.
   - `endless guide --list` prints all section slugs (no `index`).
   - `endless guide bogus-section` errors with the section list.
   - `endless quick-start` returns "No such command".
3. **File rename clean**: `git status` shows the old file deleted, new files added.
4. **No stale path/verb refs in editable paths**:
   - `grep -rn "guide-2026-04-15-using-endless-in-sessions" src/ docs/ demo/ Justfile` returns nothing.
   - `grep -rn "quick-start\|quick_start" src/ docs/ demo/ Justfile` returns nothing.
5. **Content accuracy**: every command shown in any section exists in current `endless --help` / `endless <group> --help`. Field-name claims verified against `endless task add/update --help`.
6. **Section index honesty**: every slug listed in `docs/guide/index.md` exists as a file in `docs/guide/`.
7. **No proper names**: `grep -rn "Mike\b" docs/guide/` returns nothing. The guide is generic across users of Endless.
8. **Task lifecycle**: mark E-1248 `verify` (not `confirmed`).

fields.title: Rename quick-start to guide; restructure as multi-section guide; revise content

fields.outcome: Renamed quick-start → guide; restructured doc into docs/guide/ with 15 section files (index + 14 sections) accessible via 'endless guide [<section>]' and 'endless guide --list'. Removed dated guide filename. Updated demo/10-channels.md and docs/TASK_RECORDING_PROMPT.md references. Generic author voice (no proper names). All section files verified to match shipping CLI surface as of 2026-05-11. Tested from worktree via 'uv run --directory ... endless guide': index print, section print, --list, unknown-section error, index-as-section rejection, and quick-start now reports 'No such command'.

Renamed quick-start → guide; restructured into docs/guide/ with 5 files (index + decisions + orchestration + reference + tasks) accessible via 'endless guide [<section>]' and 'endless guide --list'. Consolidated from initial 14 narrow sections after feedback that too many options hurts agent navigation. Removed dated guide filename. Updated demo/10-channels.md and docs/TASK_RECORDING_PROMPT.md references. Generic author voice (no proper names). Uses 'esu' helper (which ships in shell-init today); E-1180 tracks adding 'eswt' which will warrant a guide revisit. All commands verified from worktree.

payload.title: Consider renaming task statuses from 'needs_plan' and 'is_progress' to 'pending' and one of 'started', 'underway', 'active', 'working', 'implementing' or 'executing'
Fix right-click menu to anchor at mouse cursor (capture coords at binding time)
payload.description: Deeper fix for the right-click menu position bug that E-1251 only superficially addressed. With E-1246`s refactor, bindings do `run-shell endless-tmux show-menu --position=mouse`, and my Go binary then invokes `tmux display-menu -x M -y S`. The `M` token is only resolvable in tmux`s original binding context — by the time `display-menu` runs from a fresh invocation inside `run-shell`, the mouse event is gone, so `M` falls back to top-left. Fix: capture `#{mouse_x}` and `#{mouse_y}` at binding time (tmux substitutes them while it still has the mouse event) and pass as numeric `--mouse-x` / `--mouse-y` flags to show-menu, which forwards them as `display-menu -x <num> -y <num>`. Bindings need double-quoted run-shell args so tmux substitutes `#{...}`.

payload.title: Fix task claim output to recommend esu instead of eswt
payload.description: task_cmd.py:2030-2039 probes for an 'eswt' shell function and recommends running 'eval "$(endless shell-init)"' with the comment 'Adds eswt shell helper func'. But shell-init does not currently ship eswt; it ships esu/esp/esf. The recommendation is therefore misleading until E-1180 lands. Change the output to recommend 'esu' (which is shipping and works today: cd to the session worktree + export ENDLESS_SESSION_ID). If E-1180 later ships eswt for pure-cd, task claim output can stay on esu (the default we want most agents to use post-claim) or get a third branch — that's a design call for E-1180's plan.

fields.outcome:
Bindings now capture `#{mouse_x}` and `#{mouse_y}` at binding time and forward them to `endless-tmux show-menu --mouse-x= --mouse-y=`. The Go binary passes them as numeric tmux display-menu coordinates instead of the `M` shorthand (which doesn't work once we shell out through run-shell).

Required side change: the right-click `run-shell` arg switched from single-quoted to double-quoted so tmux processes `#{...}` substitutions. Single quotes would have passed the literal text through.

Defensive fallback: when mouse coords are empty (e.g. someone invokes show-menu --position=mouse from a non-mouse context), buildDisplayMenuArgs uses "S" instead of crashing — menu appears adjacent to status line rather than at top-left.

Verification deferred to land+apply: prefix+e centered (unchanged), right-click on row-0 status-right should anchor at the cursor.

payload.title: Fix right-click menu vertical position to anchor at status line
payload.description: Follow-up to E-1253: after the mouse_x capture fix, the menu anchors horizontally correctly but flips to the TOP of the screen instead of staying near the click. tmux interprets `-y <mouse_y>` as "place menu top at this row"; when the click is near the bottom (status bar), the menu cannot extend below, and tmux auto-flips to the top rather than shifting up. Fix: use `-y S` (line adjacent to status bar) regardless of the click Y. The X stays as the captured numeric mouse_x. Effectively this restores E-1236`s `-x M -y S` behavior with M correctly captured at binding time.

fields.outcome:
Dropped numeric mouse_y from the right-click Y position; use `-y S` instead, which anchors the menu adjacent to the status line where the click actually was. X stays as the captured numeric mouseX.

Restores E-1236's effective placement (`-x M -y S`) with the M correctly captured at binding time so it survives the run-shell indirection introduced in E-1246.

Verification deferred to land+apply: right-click on row-0 status-right should now anchor at the cursor column AND vertically near the status bar (not top of screen).

fields.description: Replace 'endless quick-start' with 'endless guide [<section>]' accepting an optional section slug and --list flag. Move the session guide from a date-stamped single file to docs/guide/ with one file per section. Update content to reflect changes since the original 2026-04-15 doc (worktrees-per-task, task-start to task-claim rename, decisions as first-class items, commit-to-main policy, tmux integration, etc.) and strip citations to project-internal task IDs so other-user installs read cleanly. Final structure: 5 sections (index + decisions + orchestration + reference + tasks).

payload.title: Bind session to a task without altering task status (display-only claim)
payload.description: Today `endless task claim <id>` refuses when the task is in `assumed` or `confirmed` (it would demote status to `in_progress`). `--force` is the documented escape hatch but it confirms the demotion — wrong for cases where the user wants the session bound for status-bar display only, with the task left at its current status. Add either `--display-only` (claim updates sessions.active_task_id without touching tasks.status) or a separate `endless session bind <task>` verb that does the same. Use case surfaced 2026-05-11 when E-1240 was in `assumed` and the Claude window working on it could not get the status bar to show the task.

payload.title: Show contextual hints on Endless status row when session is underconfigured
payload.description: Today the second status row renders a dim placeholder dot whenever no active task is found for the focused windows panes. Replace the placeholder with a short suggestion that nudges the user toward the action that would make the bar populated. Two states to detect: (1) pane has an Endless session but `active_task_id IS NULL` — show `claim a task →`. (2) pane has Claude running (`pane_current_command == claude` per `tmux display-message -p -t <pane> #{pane_current_command}`) but no `sessions` row for that pane — show `register session →`. Otherwise keep todays dim placeholder. Both hints must be short to fit the row. Subsumes the previously proposed separate hint tasks.

payload.title: Add menu option to toggle Endless status row visibility on/off
payload.description: Let the user collapse the second status row entirely (back to `status 1`) while keeping the popup menu bindings (prefix+e and right-click on row-0 status-right) working. Add a menu item `Hide Endless row` that runs `tmux set-option -g status 1`; when the row is hidden, the same menu item flips to `Show Endless row` (runs `status 2`). Tmux `status` is server-scoped so this is global on/off, not per-window — the auto-collapse-when-empty case is filed separately as a later task.

fields.outcome: Audit verdict: FindWorktreeRoot (internal/monitor/worktree_lock.go:212-245) contains the architectural bug Mike anticipated. The dir==root termination check fires at line 235, AFTER the companion-existence check at line 229. If a companion file exists at projectRoot for any reason, the function returns projectRoot as a worktree, causing handleWorktreeAdoption to claim .endless/worktree.lock at main — exactly the failure observed during E-1209 setup pre-E-1218. RECOMMENDED FIX: move the dir==root check to the start of each loop iteration so projectRoot is never stat'd for a companion. Existing tests at lines 146-206 covered no-companion-anywhere and stray-above-projectRoot cases but missed companion-at-projectRoot. Two new tests added in implementation: TestFindWorktreeRoot_IgnoresCompanionAtProjectRoot and TestFindWorktreeRoot_PrefersRealWorktreeOverStrayAtProjectRoot. Fix is implemented and verified on E-1238's branch (task/1238-apply-projectroot-fix, commit 1db7c37). This task's branch (task/1219-audit-find-worktree-root, commit 65ce38b) carries the audit plan only. Related follow-ups filed: E-1237 (cross-project walk-up leak audit), E-1239 (companion auto-creation gap on endless task start).

payload.title: Hide Endless status row automatically when no relevant content for visible panes

payload.title: Register verbs automatically via claude haiku when task add rejects a title
payload.description: On verb-validation failure, endless task add shells out to 'claude --model haiku -p' asking whether the title's first word is a verb. If YES, parse the definition and auto-register the verb before proceeding; if NO, fall through to the current error. Removes the agent's bypass option (rewriting the title with a different verb) which has been wasting tokens across sessions — the same proposed verbs keep getting rejected and rewritten.
payload.text:
# Register verbs automatically via claude haiku when task add rejects a title

## Problem
`task add` rejects titles whose first word isn't a registered verb. Claude
sessions take the path of least resistance — rewriting the title instead of
registering the verb — because rewriting is one command and `verb add` requires
writing a definition. This repeats across sessions: the same agents propose the
same verbs and get the same rejection, wasting tokens.

Three transcripts in 2026-05 sessions confirmed the pattern (bind, pass,
auto-collapse). A fourth instance occurred during the very session in which
this task was being designed: the assistant noted that `register` was not a
registered verb, then rewrote the proposed title to use `automate` instead of
registering `register`.

Stronger error wording was considered and rejected — the existing message is
already direct and was ignored three times.

## Solution
On verb-validation failure, `task add` shells out to `claude --model haiku -p`
with a strict prompt asking whether the title's first word is a verb. If YES,
parse the definition and auto-register the verb (same effect as `endless verb
add`), print a user-visible notice, and proceed with task creation. If NO,
fall through to the current error.

## Design

### Validator prompt (verbatim)
```
Is '<word>' a verb? If it is say 'YES:' and then provide a definition. Your
definition should be a single statement that does NOT include the verb in the
statement. If not, just reply 'NO'
```

Tested on haiku — clean output. Apfel was tested and rejected (it replied YES
while explaining the word was a noun).

### Execution
- Go: `exec.Command("claude", "--model", "haiku", "-p", prompt)`. Direct PATH
  lookup bypasses shell aliases/helpers — the user's `claude` shell wrapper
  that waits on channel-approval will not be hit. No `command claude` needed
  because we are not going through a shell at all.
- Runs only on a cache miss (first time a verb is seen). Latency is invisible
  against task-add's existing cost.

### Response handling
- Trim whitespace, check `YES:` prefix → take the rest as the definition.
- Anything else (`NO`, garbled, non-zero exit) → return the standard verb-
  rejection error to the caller.

### Validator scope
Validator validates the one word it's given — it does not propose alternates.
For hyphenated compounds like `auto-collapse` where haiku correctly says NO,
the calling agent rewrites the title (current behavior). The rewrite typically
uses a single verb like `collapse`, which auto-registers cleanly. No special
hyphenation handling.

### Failure modes
- `claude` not on PATH → error tells the user to install claude. Unlikely from
  inside a Claude session.
- Haiku returns non-`YES:` for a real verb → falls through to standard error.
  Caller can `--force` or rewrite.
- Haiku returns `YES:` for a non-verb → spurious registration. Low cost; verb
  list grows. Verbs only need to be recognizable as actions for human
  readability — periodic curation is not a goal.

### User-visible output on success
```
Auto-registered verb 'bind': to fasten or attach two things together.
• Added E-NNNN: Bind sibling-pane Claude session on 'task claim' ...
```

### Implementation notes
- Auto-register codepath reuses internal verb-add logic, not a shell-out to
  `endless verb add`.
- `verbs.json` is a global-config artifact; existing auto-commit-to-main
  policy applies.

## Verification
- `endless task add 'Strengthen the X' ...` in a scratch project where
  `strengthen` isn't registered → expect auto-register notice + task created.
- `endless task add 'Frobnicate the X' ...` → expect standard verb-rejection
  error.
- `endless task add 'Auto-collapse the X' ...` → haiku says NO → standard
  error.
- `endless verb list` shows newly registered verbs after success cases.

payload.title: Auto-register via claude haiku — not apfel (returns logically inconsistent output: replied 'YES' while explaining the word was a noun), not stronger error wording (existing message is already direct and was ignored three times by Claude sessions in 2026-05, including one self-demonstrating instance during the design conversation). Auto-registering removes the agent's decision point entirely, which is the only fix that survives lazy compliance. Latency is acceptable because the validator runs only on cache miss (first time a verb is seen) and task-add is infrequent.

payload.title: Convert verbs.json to verbs.jsonl with union merge driver
payload.description: Pretty-printed JSON arrays end with ']' on a single line. When two branches (main + worktree) both append verbs, git's line-based 3-way merge conflicts on the trailing region even though the operation is purely additive (demonstrated landing E-1264). Switch to JSONL (one object per line) and add 'merge=union' in .gitattributes so git auto-concatenates both sides' new lines. Existing dedup-on-load logic absorbs any same-verb duplicates. Aligns with the existing JSONL convention used by .endless/db-ledger/.
payload.text:
# Convert verbs.json to verbs.jsonl with union merge driver

## Problem
Pretty-printed JSON arrays end with `]` on a single line. When two branches
(main and a worktree) both append verb entries, git's line-based 3-way merge
sees competing edits to the same trailing region and conflicts even though
the operation is purely additive. The existing E-1141 dedup-and-bundle step
in `_dedup_worktree_verbs_against_main` mitigates this but does not fully
prevent the textual conflict at rebase time — demonstrated when landing
E-1264 against a main that had picked up new verbs concurrently.

## Solution
Switch the on-disk format from a JSON array to JSONL (one object per line),
and add a `merge=union` entry in `.gitattributes` so git auto-concatenates
both sides' new lines without manual resolution.

Duplicates produced by the union merge (same `value` added on both sides)
are silently absorbed by the existing dedup-on-load logic in
`_add_verb_to_file` (matchers.py:836–838).

## Design

### File format

Before:
```json
[
  {"value": "add", "definition": "to introduce..."},
  {"value": "fix", "definition": "to repair..."}
]
```

After (`.endless/verbs.jsonl` + machine `verbs.jsonl`):
```
{"value": "add", "definition": "to introduce..."}
{"value": "fix", "definition": "to repair..."}
```

### .gitattributes
```
.endless/verbs.jsonl merge=union
```

### Code changes
- `src/endless/matchers.py`:
  - `project_verbs_path()` / `machine_verbs_path()` → return `verbs.jsonl`.
  - `_load_verbs_list(path)` → read line-by-line, parse each as JSON.
  - `_save_verbs_list(path, verbs)` → write line-by-line.
  - Migration: at load time, if `verbs.json` exists at the same dir, read
    its array, merge into the JSONL, then delete `verbs.json`.
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py`:
  - `AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS`: replace `.endless/verbs.json` with `.endless/verbs.jsonl`.
  - `_read_verbs_list` / `_dedup_worktree_verbs_against_main`: read/write JSONL.
  - Dedup logic remains valuable as a belt-and-suspenders measure (it still
    produces a clean bundle commit), but the union-merge fallback is now the
    primary safety net.

### Tests
- Update existing tests that assert `verbs.json` content to assert
  `verbs.jsonl`.
- Add migration test: pre-existing `verbs.json` is read and converted to
  `verbs.jsonl` on next load.
- Update `test_worktree_land_dedup.py` to use JSONL.

## Verification
- `endless verb list` continues to show all verbs after migration.
- `endless verb add <new>` writes a single line to `verbs.jsonl`.
- Concurrent verb adds on two branches followed by rebase auto-merges (no
  manual resolution).
- Existing `verbs.json` is migrated automatically on first load.
- `just test` passes.

fields.text:
# E-987 — Resolve event-log auto-append vs clean-main tension

> Part of E-968 (parallel-session support). Companion to E-971 (the worktrees + behavioral gate work that needs `land` to refuse dirty main).
>
> **Status**: design locked 2026-04-30. Implementation pending.

## The conflict

Two prior designs collide:

1. **E-808 (commit c9425fb)** made the event log authoritative. Every endless CLI mutation appends to `.endless/events/events-*.jsonl`. The events log is committed and replayable.
2. **E-971 (planned)** says main is the sacred clean integration target. `endless worktree land` refuses dirty main.

Per E-972, all events route to main's `.endless/events/` regardless of which worktree the session is in. So every CLI invocation leaves main dirty. Without resolution, every `land` call would refuse.

## Resolution: Option F (auto-commit endless-managed files independently)

Define **endless-managed files** (a configurable list) that `land` auto-commits as a separate step BEFORE attempting the worktree merge. Independent commit, not bundled into the worktree's land commit.

### Auto-commit list (locked)

| Path | Why it's auto-managed | Source |
|---|---|---|
| `.endless/events/*.jsonl` | Append-only log, written on every mutation | E-808 |
| `.endless/plans/snapshots/*.md` | Write-once historical record per plan-file write | E-969 |
| `.endless/plans/snapshots/*.json` | Sidecar metadata for above | E-969 |

### Explicitly NOT in the auto-commit list

| Path | Why it's user-work, not auto |
|---|---|
| `.endless/config.json` | User edits matter (E-970 matchers added/disabled by user action) |
| `.endless/plans/<task-id>.md` | Per-task plan content; user-attached |
| `.endless/data.sql` | Periodic dump created by explicit command, not automatic. See E-1018 for the data.sql lifecycle decision (deferred). |
| Any source code | Obviously user-work |

### Configurability

Hardcoded defaults in endless. Project can extend via `.endless/config.json` `auto_commit_paths` array. Project cannot REMOVE defaults (one-way override) so the architecture's invariants stay intact.

## Land flow (with retry loop)

```
MAX_RETRIES = 8

for attempt in 1..MAX_RETRIES:
    auto_files_dirty = git_status_match(AUTO_COMMIT_PATHS)
    user_files_dirty = git_status_minus(AUTO_COMMIT_PATHS)

    if user_files_dirty:
        refuse with actionable message:
          "main has uncommitted user changes; commit them, move them to a
           worktree, or set them aside before landing E-XXX. Files: ..."

    if auto_files_dirty:
        git add <auto paths>
        git commit -m "Endless: auto-record session activity"

    try:
        cd <worktree>
        git rebase main
        cd <main>
        git merge --ff-only <worktree-branch>
        git worktree remove <worktree-path>
        return success

    except blocked-by-dirty-events-log:
        # Concurrent writer raced us; retry the auto-commit step
        continue

fail with:
    "another session is appending to events log faster than land can
     converge after MAX_RETRIES attempts; try again later."
```

### Why rebase, not merge commit

- **Linear history**: per-task commits land on top of the auto-commit, no merge bubbles.
- **Conflict surface**: rebase against an auto-commit is virtually never problematic because (per E-972) auto-managed files are written to main's location only — worktree branches don't modify them. Rebase has nothing to conflict on.
- **Explicit identity**: the auto-commit appears as a distinct commit in `git log`, named clearly (`Endless: auto-record session activity`). Easy to skip when reviewing per-task work.

### Race condition handling

The retry loop addresses concurrent writers:

```
T0: Land begins; auto-commit step picks up events at state-X
T1: Session B (concurrent) appends event Y to events log
T2: Land attempts rebase / ff-merge
T3: Merge refuses because events log is dirty again
T4: Loop: re-run auto-commit step (now picks up event Y too)
T5: Retry merge — succeeds
```

`MAX_RETRIES = 8` is a sane cap. Each iteration is fast (one commit, one merge attempt). If we exceed the cap, something pathological is happening (busy loop of writes); fail with a message and let the user retry later.

## Hand-edit prohibition (cross-reference)

E-1017 (decision, confirmed) makes hand-editing of endless-managed files forbidden. Auto-managed files are write-once-by-endless. This is what makes the rebase-conflict-free property reliable: nothing should EVER produce conflicting writes on these files from a worktree.

## Recovery from architectural drift

When the rebase DOES conflict on an auto-managed file (a future-feature bug, a pre-E-972 worktree, a malicious hand-edit), `land` fails with the conflict. v1 documents the recovery procedure as part of the error message:

```
git rebase --abort
git checkout main -- .endless/events/ .endless/plans/snapshots/
endless worktree land <task-id>  # retry
```

E-1019 captures this and the eventual `--resolve-auto` automation if it ever bites.

## Critical files

- `internal/git/worktree.go` (new in E-971; this work extends it) — auto-commit step + retry loop logic.
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` (new in E-971; this work extends it) — `endless worktree land` CLI implementation.
- `internal/monitor/config.go` or equivalent — read `auto_commit_paths` from config (hardcoded defaults + project overrides).
- Documentation (CLAUDE.md or similar) — reference E-1017's hand-edit rule near any code that writes to events log or snapshots.

## Verification

1. **Happy path**: clean main + uncommitted events appends + worktree branch with task work → `land` auto-commits events, ff-merges successfully, worktree removed.
2. **User-work blocks**: dirty user-work file (e.g., source code) on main → `land` refuses with actionable message.
3. **Concurrent writer race**: while `land` runs, append to events log via another session → loop iterates → succeeds within a few retries.
4. **Pathological race**: simulate continuous appends from another session → `land` fails after MAX_RETRIES with the bounded-loop error.
5. **Architectural drift conflict**: hand-edit events log in worktree (violating E-1017), then `land` → rebase conflict, error message includes recovery commands.

## Out of scope

- data.sql lifecycle (Pattern A vs B): E-1018, deferred.
- Recovery automation (`--resolve-auto`): E-1019, deferred until needed.
- Cross-platform git portability: use `git rev-parse` and standard porcelain commands; no shell-specific logic.



---

## Addendum (2026-05-11): E-1240 demonstrated the unsafe-recovery-hint case

The "Recovery from architectural drift" section above documents v1's procedure for handling rebase conflicts on auto-managed files:

```
git rebase --abort
git checkout main -- .endless/events/ .endless/plans/snapshots/
endless worktree land <task-id>  # retry
```

That procedure assumes the worktree's writes to auto-managed files were **unintentional** — ambient dirt that should be discarded. E-1240 demonstrated a case the procedure does not handle:

- E-1240 added a new optional `completable` schema field to `.endless/verbs.json` plus 7 new verb entries. All intentional, all part of the feature.
- During `endless worktree land E-1240`, the rebase hit a conflict on `verbs.json` because main's auto-record had separately appended a new verb (`bind`).
- The stock recovery hint (now also surfaced on the `db-ledger` and `verbs.json` paths) said to `git checkout main -- .endless/verbs.json` and retry — which would have **silently destroyed E-1240's intentional schema additions**.

The conflict was resolved by hand (kept both sides). But the recovery hint is unsafe as a general procedure: it cannot tell "ambient auto-dirt" from "intentional feature content on an auto-managed file."

### Design questions opened (carried forward to E-1019)

1. **Detection.** How does `worktree land` differentiate the two cases? Candidate signals:
   - Commit metadata: was the worktree-side conflicting commit authored by the auto-record path (commit message starts with "Endless: auto-record session activity")?
   - Content-shape: did the worktree add NEW top-level keys / NEW array entries / NEW schema fields, vs. only mutate values of pre-existing entries?
   - Explicit feature-intent marker on the commit
   - Whether the file is touched by other (non-auto-record) commits on the worktree branch
2. **Action.** When the unsafe case is detected, what should `worktree land` do?
   - Refuse with a clear message
   - Drop into an interactive structural-merge helper (verbs.json has known schema — see `worktree_cmd._dedup_worktree_verbs_against_main` from E-1141/E-1138, which already does value-level merges for the auto-commit path)
   - Reuse the dedup helper for the rebase path
   - Document only
3. **Scope split.** Does this expand E-1019, file a sibling task under E-968, or split across both? Note: this design's analysis above is scoped to event logs (append-only). verbs.json is structured-with-schema, so the right design may diverge.
4. **Hand-edit prohibition.** E-1017 forbids hand-editing endless-managed files. E-1240 is **not** a hand-edit — it's a feature commit that legitimately modified the schema. The prohibition needs to either explicitly carve out "feature-intent" schema changes, or the auto-managed file set needs to grow a notion of "this is structured config that features may legitimately modify."

### Why this changes the v1 plan

The "Recovery from architectural drift" v1 procedure is correct *for event logs*. The error message that surfaces it must not promote that procedure for **any** auto-managed file uniformly — it's actively dangerous for `verbs.json`. Either the error message must be file-specific (event logs → checkout-from-main is safe; verbs.json → structural merge required), or the recovery must be one step removed from a copy-pasteable command.

fields.text:
# E-987 — Resolve event-log auto-append vs clean-main tension

> Part of E-968 (parallel-session support). Research task; deliverable is this design text.
>
> **Status**: resolved 2026-05-11. Implementation handed off to follow-up tasks (see below).

## The original conflict

Two prior designs collided when E-987 was originally filed:

1. **E-808** made the event log authoritative. Every endless CLI mutation appended to `.endless/events/events-*.jsonl` (renamed by E-1197 to `.endless/db-ledger/db-entries-*.jsonl`). The ledger is committed and replayable.
2. **E-971** declared `main` the sacred clean integration target. `endless worktree land` refuses dirty `main`.

Per E-972, all writes route to `main`'s `.endless/` directory regardless of which worktree the session is in. So every CLI invocation left `main` dirty, and without resolution every `land` call would refuse.

The v1 design (locked 2026-04-30) chose **auto-commit-at-land**: `land` auto-committed any dirt matching an `AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS` set, as a separate `Endless: auto-record session activity` commit, before rebasing the worktree branch onto `main`. It also documented a recovery procedure for the rare case where the rebase conflicted on an auto-managed file — the documented procedure was `git rebase --abort && git checkout main -- <auto-paths> && retry land`.

## What the addendum surfaced (2026-05-11)

E-1240 (Add completed status gated by verbs.json completable flag) demonstrated a case the v1 recovery procedure mishandled. E-1240's feature commit legitimately added new entries and a new schema field to `.endless/verbs.json`. `endless worktree land E-1240` hit a rebase conflict because `main` had auto-recorded a separate verb addition (`bind`). The stock recovery hint would have told the user to `git checkout main -- .endless/verbs.json` — which would have silently destroyed E-1240's intentional schema additions. The conflict was resolved by hand.

The addendum opened four design questions; this section records their resolution.

## Architectural correction (the resolution)

The deeper issue the addendum surfaced is that **auto-commit-at-land is the wrong steady-state mechanism**. The v1 plan absorbed dirt on `main` to keep `land` working. That absorbs bugs: errant search-and-replace, agent error, user edits the user did not realize had been left behind. Those should never be silently committed.

Correct framing:

1. **`main` should never be dirty in steady state.** Once endless is hardened, neither endless nor Claude should leave `main` dirty. Dirt is a symptom, not normal state.
2. **`land` should NOT auto-commit anything except snapshots.** Db-ledger and verbs.jsonl writes get committed at the *point of write*, not at land. Snapshots stay auto-committed at land because they're posterity (write-once historical record), not transactional state.
3. **Auto-managed files are tiered by sacredness, not uniformly prohibited:**
   - `.endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl` — **sacred**. Hand-edits only as a last-resort merge-conflict resolution.
   - `.endless/plans/snapshots/*` — **mildly sacred**. Captured history; hand-edit not a crisis.
   - `.endless/verbs.jsonl` — **not sacred**. Actively maintained; hand-edits on `main` (if committed) are fine.
4. **The current recovery hint at `worktree_cmd.py:744–759` is actively unsafe** and must be replaced. The replacement is a "investigate this rebase conflict" message that names the conflicted paths and points at E-972's routing invariant — no destructive copy-paste recipe.

## Successor tasks (where the work goes)

Already filed and pre-existing:

| Task   | Status         | What it does for the resolution                                                                                |
|--------|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| E-1197 | verify         | Renamed `.endless/events/` → `.endless/db-ledger/`. Path-rename predicate for everything else here.            |
| E-1268 | confirmed      | Converted `verbs.json` → `verbs.jsonl` with `merge=union` in `.gitattributes`. Closes the E-1240 textual conflict scenario. |
| E-1206 | needs_plan     | Auto-commit each `.endless/db-ledger/` write immediately. Removes db-ledger from `land`'s auto-commit scope.   |
| E-1208 | needs_plan     | Auto-commit `verbs.jsonl` immediately on `endless verb add`. Removes verbs.jsonl from `land`'s auto-commit scope. |
| E-1216 | needs_plan     | Move plan-file writes from `main` to the active task worktree. Plan files stop being a `main`-dirt source.      |
| E-1207 | needs_plan     | Validate plan-file path against the task's worktree on `task update --text`. Enforcement layer for E-1216.     |
| E-1202 | needs_plan     | Forbid plan files at `.endless/plans/E-NNN.md` in `main`'s working tree. Hardens E-1216.                       |
| E-1061 | later, needs_plan | Generic post-edit commit prompt for any CLI mutating auto-committed files. Backstop for the broader pattern.|

Filed as part of this resolution (see "Resolution actions" below):

- **Bundled `land` change** — narrow `land`'s auto-commit to snapshots only; replace the destructive recovery hint with a non-destructive investigate message. Blocked on E-1206 and E-1208 so it doesn't ship before write-time auto-commit is in place.
- **merge=union shipping** — ensure `verbs.jsonl merge=union` in `.gitattributes` ships with v1.0 and design an in-Go merge fallback for projects that can't take the gitattributes change.
- **E-1017 rename** — update E-1017's decision text to use `db-ledger/` instead of the old `events/`.

## Resolution of the four addendum questions

### Q1 — Detection of feature-intent vs auto-record on rebase conflict

The partitioned-by-file-class detection with commit-subject probing was the wrong shape. E-1268's `merge=union` closed the headline E-1240 case. E-972's routing rule means db-ledger and snapshots can't legitimately have worktree-side commits. The broader shift (auto-commit at write time) removes the substrate for these conflicts altogether.

**Decision:** decline the partitioned-detection scope. Replace the recovery hint with a single "investigate" message naming the conflicted paths and the E-972 invariant.

### Q2 — db-ledger / snapshot conflict action

**Decision:** refuse with "routing bug — investigate." Per E-972 these shouldn't occur from a worktree side; auto-discarding worktree-side content silently destroys the only evidence of where routing broke. The replacement hint will name this directly.

This does not contradict the operational reality that snapshots and verbs.jsonl *can* be hand-edited on `main` when committed. The "routing bug" framing applies specifically to **worktree-side commits** on db-ledger / snapshots, which contradict E-972.

### Q3 — Scope split

**Decision:** decline E-1019 (it had been chartered for the v1 recovery procedure; that scope is no longer right). File the bundled `land` change as a new task under E-968.

### Q4 — E-1017 hand-edit prohibition wording

**Decision:** no textual carve-out for feature commits. The "hand-edit" wording covers manual edits, and Mike's operational tiering — db-ledger sacred, snapshots mildly sacred, verbs.jsonl not sacred — is operational guidance rather than a wording change. E-1017's text continues to apply to manual `vim` edits; programmatic commits from a worktree branch were never going to be the path for legitimate feature work anyway.

E-1017's text still uses the old `events/` directory name; that's filed as a standalone rename follow-up.

## Resolution actions taken on this task

1. This text replaces the v1 plan body and the 2026-05-11 addendum.
2. E-1019 transitioned to `declined` with reason recorded.
3. Follow-up tasks filed: (a) E-1017 events→db-ledger rename, (b) bundled `land` narrowing + non-destructive hint, (c) merge=union shipping / in-Go fallback.
4. This task transitions to `completed` (or `assumed` if the gate rejects).

## Critical files (for the follow-up tasks, not this one)

- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:53–58` — `AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS`. The bundled follow-up reduces this to snapshots-only.
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:702–730` — current auto-commit step at `land`. The bundled follow-up narrows it.
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:744–759` — current destructive recovery hint. The bundled follow-up replaces it.
- `.gitattributes` — `merge=union` entry for `verbs.jsonl`. The merge=union shipping task ensures it ships with v1.0 and designs the in-Go fallback.
- E-1017's decision text — the standalone rename task updates it.

## What is explicitly NOT in scope going forward

- Per-file commit-subject probing on rebase conflict (was Q1's candidate detection signal). Wrong shape after the architectural shift.
- Auto-discard of worktree-side content for db-ledger / snapshots. Per Q2, refuse and surface.
- Carve-outs in E-1017's hand-edit prohibition text. Wording is fine; operational tiering lives in this document and the follow-up tasks.
- Auto-commit-at-land for db-ledger / verbs.jsonl. Those move to write-time per E-1206 / E-1208.

Extend it (or add an immediately-following step) to git-add and commit the just-written snapshot pair. Commit subject suggestion: 'Endless: capture plan snapshot <stem>' or similar. Goal: after E-1206, E-1208, and this task land, nothing endless-managed remains dirty on main between writes — worktree land's auto-commit step has nothing to do.

Drop the entire auto-commit step in worktree_cmd.py:702-730 (the AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS partitioning + commit) — non-snapshot dirt on main becomes an error path treated like any other user-work dirt: refuse with an actionable message. (b) Replace the destructive 'git checkout main -- ...' recovery hint at worktree_cmd.py:744-759 with a non-destructive 'investigate this rebase conflict' message that names the conflicted paths and points at E-972's routing invariant. Blocked on E-1206 / E-1208 / E-1275 because dropping the auto-commit step before write-time commits land would make routine appends fail at land.

fields.text:
# E-1208 — Record verbs.jsonl changes immediately on endless verb add

## Context

Today `endless verb add <name>` (and the auto-registration path from `task_cmd.validate_title`) writes a new entry into `<project>/.endless/verbs.jsonl` but does **not** commit. The file is left dirty in the working tree until either someone manually commits or the next `endless worktree land` bundles it into the auto-record commit.

Both outcomes are wrong:
- Manual-commit creates a coordination tax across sessions.
- Bundling at land couples a global-config event (registering a verb) to an unrelated worktree-land timing.

Per E-987's resolution, **write-time auto-commit** is the correct pattern for endless-managed files. Today's dirt was visible in this session: `.endless/verbs.jsonl` is modified on main because the in-flight `task add` calls auto-registered the verbs `narrow` and `ensure`.

The task description (and E-1199's global-config exception) authorize **direct commit to main, bypassing the task/worktree association**: the verb registry is global config, and the commit travels with main regardless of which working tree the calling session was in.

## What's actually being changed

Two behavior changes ride together:

1. **Route the project verbs.jsonl write to main's path.** Today `matchers.project_verbs_path()` walks up from cwd, so a worktree session writes to the worktree's `.endless/verbs.jsonl`. The replacement walks the registered-project record (the same path resolution `worktree_cmd._project_root()` already does) so writes always target main's tree, regardless of cwd.
2. **Commit the file immediately after a successful write.** Single-file `git add` + `git commit` on main's repo. Skipped if the write was a no-op (verb already present). Machine-layer writes are not touched — `~/.config/endless/verbs.jsonl` is not git-tracked.

Together these mean: a verb registration from any session lands as one commit on main with a message like `Endless: register verb 'foo'`, and the next session in any worktree picks it up via the read path (which also routes to main).

## Code surface

**Files to modify:**
- `src/endless/matchers.py:113` — `project_verbs_path()`. Change resolution from cwd-walk-up to registered-project-path lookup. Use the same pattern as `worktree_cmd._project_root()` (`_resolve_project(None)` → DB lookup → `path` column). Returns `None` when no registered project, preserving existing `None`-handling at callers.
- `src/endless/matchers.py:858` — `add_verb()`. After a successful project-side `_add_verb_to_file` (returns `True`), call a new private helper `_commit_project_verbs(verb_value)`. No commit when `wrote_project=False` (no-op) or when no registered project.
- `src/endless/matchers.py` (new helper) — `_commit_project_verbs(verb_value: str)`. Resolves main's registered path, then runs a single command: `git -C <main> commit -o .endless/verbs.jsonl -m "Endless: register verb '<verb_value>'"`. The `-o` (`--only`) flag tells git to stage **just** the working-tree content of that one path and commit, leaving the rest of the index untouched. Other dirt on main (staged or unstaged) is preserved as-is and does not block our commit. On subprocess failure, raises `RuntimeError` with the git stderr. **Imports** `_project_root` and `_git_run` from `endless.worktree_cmd` (no cycle: worktree_cmd doesn't import matchers).
- `src/endless/verb_cmd.py:15` — `add_verb`. Wrap the `matchers.add_verb` call to catch `RuntimeError` and re-raise as `click.ClickException` (preserves non-zero exit semantics per Mike's failure-handling answer).
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py:299` — auto-register path. **No catch added.** The existing `except ValueError: pass` catches validation errors only; a `RuntimeError` from a commit failure propagates naturally, surfacing as a `task add` failure (non-zero exit). Per Mike: he wants to see what kinds of errors actually occur before deciding which to soften.

**Tests to update or add:**
- `tests/test_verb_gate.py:63` — existing `test_verb_add_persists_in_verbs_file` uses `machine_only=True` and doesn't exercise the new path. Keep as-is.
- New test (`test_verb_add_commits_to_main`): with a temp git repo and registered project, call `verb_cmd.add_verb` for a new verb. Assert (a) `.endless/verbs.jsonl` has the entry, (b) `git status` is clean for that file, (c) HEAD's commit message matches `Endless: register verb '<v>'`.
- New test (`test_verb_add_no_commit_on_noop`): add the same verb twice. Assert HEAD advances exactly once.
- New test (`test_verb_add_machine_only_no_commit`): with machine_only=True in a git repo, assert HEAD does NOT advance.
- New test (`test_verb_add_commit_failure_raises`): simulate commit failure (e.g., make .git read-only after the file write, or stub `_git_run` to raise). Assert `ClickException` from `verb_cmd.add_verb` and that the file write persisted.
- New test (`test_auto_register_propagates_commit_failure`): from `task_cmd.validate_title`, with auto-register triggering and the commit failing, assert the `RuntimeError` (or the wrapped exception) propagates — `task add` itself fails. Verify the file write happened first.

## Failure handling

The file write happens first, then the `git commit -o` for that one path. Decisions:

| Failure                                              | Response                                               |
|------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| No registered project (project_verbs_path() → None)  | Machine-only write succeeds; no commit attempted; no error. |
| `git commit -o <path>` fails (any reason)            | `matchers.add_verb` raises `RuntimeError` with the git stderr included. Both call sites — the CLI (`verb_cmd.add_verb`) and the auto-register path (`task_cmd.validate_title`) — surface the failure as a non-zero exit. The file write persisted (verb is in `verbs.jsonl`), but the working tree is dirty and the caller is informed. Per Mike: fail loudly everywhere until we know what failure modes look like in practice. |
| Concurrent writer raced us between write and commit  | Acceptable: `commit -o <path>` only stages and commits that one path. Another writer concurrently adding a different verb does its own `commit -o`. Both commits land back-to-back (or git's ref-update lock serializes them). |
| Dirty main at commit time (user has other changes)   | Not a failure mode. `commit -o` does not consult or disturb other files' index/working-tree state. The user's in-flight work is preserved. |

No rollback on commit failure: the file has the new verb. The next CLI invocation may surface the dirt; the existing land-time fallback (`worktree_cmd`'s auto-commit step) covers the gap until E-1272 lands.

## No-registered-project case

If `project_verbs_path()` returns `None` (no registered project — typical at first install or in a non-project directory), the behavior is unchanged: machine-layer write only, no project file touched, no commit. The new commit step is conditional on the project write actually happening.

## Verification

1. **Happy path (main, clean)**: From a clean main checkout, `endless verb add ponder --definition "to deliberate over"`. Expect: `.endless/verbs.jsonl` has the new entry, working tree is clean for that file, `git log -1 --format=%s` shows `Endless: register verb 'ponder'`.
2. **Happy path (main, dirty)**: With unrelated dirt on main (e.g., a touch src/foo.py change), run the same command. Expect: same as #1 — verbs.jsonl is committed cleanly, the unrelated dirt remains exactly as it was (preserved by `commit -o`).
3. **Happy path (from worktree)**: From inside `.endless/worktrees/e-1208/`, `endless verb add musing --definition "..."`. Expect: main's verbs.jsonl (NOT the worktree's) has the new entry; main's HEAD has the new commit; the worktree's `.endless/verbs.jsonl` is unchanged on disk until the next rebase.
4. **No-op**: Same verb twice. Second call: no commit, no file change, exits with "already present" message.
5. **Machine-only**: `endless verb add foo --machine-only --definition "..."`. Expect: machine layer updated, no commit on main, project file unchanged.
6. **Auto-register propagates failure**: Stub out git so commit fails (e.g., make `.git` read-only). Run `endless task add "Mull over the design"`. Expect: task add fails non-zero with the git stderr surfaced; verbs.jsonl has the new entry persisted on disk; HEAD didn't advance.
7. **Tests**: `just test` — new tests cover scenarios 1–6 with monkeypatched temp git repos. Existing tests unaffected.

## Out of scope

- Removing the `_dedup_worktree_verbs_against_main` helper. Once E-1208 ships, the worktree should never have a divergent verbs.jsonl (writes go to main directly). The dedup helper becomes dead code, but removal lands with E-1272 (the bundled `land` change) where the entire auto-commit step is dropped.
- Reading from main when in a worktree (for non-verbs files). Out of scope; this is just verbs.
- File-write atomicity (lock-then-write). The existing read-modify-write pattern is unchanged; merge=union backstops textual races during rebase.

fields.text:
# E-1274 — Fix spawn pre-binding session to task without creating worktree

## Context

`endless task spawn E-NNN` opens a new tmux window with Claude and pastes the task's prompt. Today it sets `@endless_task_id=NNN` on the new window before launching Claude. When the spawned Claude starts, its `SessionStart` hook reads `@endless_task_id` and calls `monitor.StartWorkSession()` (cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:144-146), which in one Go function both (a) writes `sessions.active_task_id=NNN` and (b) flips `tasks.status` to `in_progress`. No events are emitted; the state change happens via direct SQL.

By the time the spawned Claude runs `endless task claim E-NNN` (per orchestration boilerplate), the session is already bound and the status is already flipped. `claim_item` calls `_check_task_ownership()` which returns True (self-bound), then short-circuits at `task_cmd.py:2134-2156` — prints "E-NNN is already active in session N" (misleading for a fresh-spawned session that thinks it's claiming for the first time), creates the worktree, returns. The full claim path that emits `task.status_changed` and `task.claimed` events is skipped.

Two parts of the original spec are no longer accurate (E-1229 landed before E-1274 was filed):
- "no worktree" — short-circuit does create the worktree as of E-1229.
- "status still ready/needs_plan" — status is flipped, by `StartWorkSession`'s direct SQL.

What remains to fix:
- The "already active in session N" message reads as an error to the spawned Claude.
- State mutations bypass the event log (no `task.status_changed`, no `task.claimed` event for the spawn-then-claim flow).
- The full claim path's help text ("To work on this task: ...") is skipped.
- `@endless_task_id` is overloaded — it means both "this window has task X in context" *and* "this window was spawned with task X as the intent". Any future code that sets `@endless_task_id` for window-context reasons would unintentionally trigger spawn-flow logic. (Mike's footgun call.)

Mike's chosen direction:
1. Spawn does the full claim (status flip + worktree) *before* launching Claude.
2. Introduce a distinct spawn marker `@endless_spawned_by=<spawner_id>`. The SessionStart hook keys off this — not `@endless_task_id` — to decide spawn-flow vs. non-spawn behavior.
3. SessionStart's spawn-flow path emits `task.claimed` for audit, and does *not* flip status (spawn already did it).
4. Use case 2 (end user starts a Claude session directly, e.g. inside a worktree via `eswt`) is *not* touched in this task — today it doesn't auto-bind from `@endless_task_id` either (no tmux marker is set), so it still works correctly via the user's explicit `endless task claim`. Adding cwd-based auto-bind for use case 2 is filed as a separate follow-up with `--cleans-up E-1274`, coordinated with E-1195 (worktree.lock removal).

## Approach

Mix of Python (spawn pre-claim, claim refactor) and Go (split `StartWorkSession`, change SessionStart's hook key).

### 1. Python: extract shared `_perform_claim_work` helper

In `src/endless/task_cmd.py`. Private helper used by both `claim_item` and `spawn_plan`:

```python
def _perform_claim_work(
    item_id: int,
    title: str,
    current_status: str,
    target_session: int | None,
    proj_name: str,
) -> tuple[Path, bool]:
    """Emit claim events and create the worktree. Returns (wt_path, created).

    Caller has already resolved target_session and validated the
    status/ownership gates. target_session=None means no session binding
    yet (spawn case: Claude hasn't started).
    """
    if current_status != "in_progress":
        emit_event(
            kind="task.status_changed",
            project=proj_name,
            entity_type="task",
            entity_id=str(item_id),
            payload={"old_status": current_status, "new_status": "in_progress"},
        )
        _emit_field_changes(item_id, title,
                            [("status", current_status, "in_progress")])

    if target_session is not None:
        emit_event(
            kind="task.claimed",
            project=proj_name,
            entity_type="task",
            entity_id=str(item_id),
            payload={"session_id": target_session},
        )

    return create_task_worktree(item_id, title or "task", _project_root())
```

The done-ish-status gate (`_CLAIM_REQUIRES_FORCE`) and the multi-owner refusal in `_check_task_ownership` stay in their respective callers (they run *before* `_perform_claim_work`).

### 2. Python: refactor `claim_item` to use the helper

Public behavior preserved. The full path delegates to `_perform_claim_work(..., target_session=<resolved>, ...)`. The short-circuit path (`_check_task_ownership` returns True — self-already-bound) stays as the friendly notice + idempotent worktree create. This keeps `claim` safe if the spawned Claude (or a user) runs it after spawn already claimed.

### 3. Python: update `spawn_plan` to do the claim work before tmux window creation

In `spawn_plan` (task_cmd.py:2726+), insert the claim work between prompt validation and `tmux new-window`:

```python
# After prompt/--worktree validation, before window creation:

# Done-ish status gate — mirror claim_item
if not force and current_status in _CLAIM_REQUIRES_FORCE:
    raise click.ClickException(...)

# Refuse if another live session already owns the task.
# Pass current_eid=None so any owner is treated as "other".
_check_task_ownership(item_id, current_eid=None)

# Resolve the spawner's identity for the spawned-by marker
spawner_id = _current_endless_session_id() or f"pid-{os.getpid()}"

# Do the claim work (no session binding yet — Claude not started)
_, proj_name = _resolve_project(None)
wt_path, _ = _perform_claim_work(
    item_id, title, current_status,
    target_session=None,
    proj_name=proj_name,
)

# Default cd_target to the new worktree (existing --worktree still overrides)
if worktree is None:
    cd_target = str(wt_path)
```

Then when creating the tmux window, set the new spawn marker alongside the existing task-id var:

```python
subprocess.run(
    ["tmux", "set", "-w", "-t", window_name,
     "@endless_spawned_by", str(spawner_id)],
    check=True,
)
subprocess.run(
    ["tmux", "set", "-w", "-t", window_name,
     "@endless_task_id", str(item_id)],
    check=True,
)
```

Add a `--force` CLI flag to `spawn` (mirrors `claim --force`).

### 4. Go: split `StartWorkSession`

In `internal/monitor/session.go`, factor today's single function into two:

```go
// BindSessionToTask records the session-task binding only.
// Does NOT change task status — caller (typically via task.claimed
// event executor or spawn's pre-claim flow) is responsible for status.
func BindSessionToTask(sessionID string, projectID int64, taskID int64) error {
    // (current StartWorkSession body, MINUS the "UPDATE tasks SET status" line)
}

// StartWorkSession binds the session AND marks the task in_progress.
// Used by the post-bash `endless task claim` detector (defense-in-depth
// alongside the Python-emitted task.status_changed event).
func StartWorkSession(sessionID string, projectID int64, taskID int64) error {
    if err := BindSessionToTask(sessionID, projectID, taskID); err != nil {
        return err
    }
    return markTaskInProgress(taskID)  // the existing UPDATE-eligible-statuses query
}
```

### 5. Go: narrow SessionStart's auto-bind to `@endless_spawned_by`

In `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:141-146`, change the keying from `@endless_task_id` to `@endless_spawned_by`. Add a `tmuxSpawnedBy()` helper paralleling the existing `tmuxTaskID()`:

```go
// In the SessionStart switch arm:
if spawnedBy := tmuxSpawnedBy(); spawnedBy != "" {
    if taskID := tmuxTaskID(); taskID > 0 {
        // Spawn flow: status already flipped by spawn's pre-claim;
        // only the binding remains. Use BindSessionToTask, not
        // StartWorkSession, to avoid a redundant status-flip path.
        monitor.BindSessionToTask(payload.SessionID, projectID, taskID)
    }
}
```

When `@endless_spawned_by` is unset (use case 2 — user-started Claude in a worktree), the spawn-flow code does not run. The session is unbound from any task; the user runs `endless task claim` as today, which exercises the full claim path. This preserves use-case 2 behavior unchanged.

The post-bash `endless task claim` detector at claude.go:597 keeps calling `StartWorkSession` (no change there — it remains the defense-in-depth mirror of Python claim_item's events).

### 6. Documentation update — `docs/guide/orchestration.md`

- Line 155 ("Spawn does *not* auto-claim the task..."): change to describe new behavior — spawn flips status and creates the worktree before launching Claude; the spawned session lands in the worktree already claimed; SessionStart records the session binding via `task.claimed` event.
- Lines 176 and 205 (the example prompt's `endless task claim` boilerplate): remove the claim step (or note it is a safe idempotent no-op).
- Add `--force` for spawn alongside `--no-plan` and `--worktree`.

### 7. File follow-up task (during implementation, not plan mode)

After the implementation lands, file:
```
endless task add "SessionStart auto-binds session to task by cwd-in-worktree (use case 2)" \
    --cleans-up E-1274 \
    --description "Today, an end-user `claude` session in a worktree only claims the filesystem lock; the task-session binding is not auto-set. Make SessionStart auto-bind sessions.active_task_id when cwd is inside an endless worktree and the task is unclaimed by a live session. Coordinate with E-1195 (worktree.lock removal) so this is built on the DB-backed binding/lock model, not the legacy lock file."
```

## Critical files

- `src/endless/task_cmd.py:2026-2222` — `claim_item`, `_check_task_ownership` (refactor; extract helper)
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py:2726-2867` — `spawn_plan` (add pre-claim, set `@endless_spawned_by`, add `--force`)
- `src/endless/cli.py` (spawn CLI option registration) — `--force` flag
- `internal/monitor/session.go:23-52` — split `StartWorkSession` into `BindSessionToTask` + `StartWorkSession`
- `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:141-146, 993-1015` — narrow auto-bind to `@endless_spawned_by`; add `tmuxSpawnedBy()` helper
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:606-658` — `create_task_worktree` (no change; already idempotent)
- `docs/guide/orchestration.md:155, 176, 205` — text updates

## Reused / not new

- `_perform_claim_work` uses existing event kinds (`task.status_changed`, `task.claimed`) and existing executor logic in `internal/events/executor.go`.
- `create_task_worktree` already returns `(path, created)` and is idempotent on existing canonical worktree.
- `_check_task_ownership` already raises with a clear message when a different live session owns the task.

## Verification

Run from the worktree (`.endless/worktrees/e-1274`):

1. **Build:** `just build` from main checkout (the recipe needs to be run from main per project rules; verify both Go and templ compile cleanly).
2. **Tests:** `just test` for Python.
3. **Smoke test — spawn a fresh task:**
   - From a Claude session in main: `endless task add "verification target" --description "scratch"` → returns E-XXXX.
   - `endless task update E-XXXX --prompt /tmp/p.md` (any short prompt).
   - `endless task spawn E-XXXX` from inside tmux. Expect spawn output to include status-flip + worktree-created lines (in addition to the window-spawned line).
   - `endless task show E-XXXX` → status is `in_progress`.
   - `endless worktree for-task E-XXXX` → returns the worktree path.
   - In the spawned tmux window: `pwd` is the worktree directory.
   - In the spawned window: `endless task claim E-XXXX` prints the friendly "already active" notice and exits cleanly (idempotent).
   - Tear down: `endless worktree drop E-XXXX --force` + `endless task update E-XXXX --status obsolete`.
4. **Refusal cases:**
   - Spawn a task in `verify` without `--force` → refused with a `claim_item`-style message.
   - Spawn the same task with `--force` → proceeds.
   - Set up a task owned by a live session in another shell, then `spawn` from your own → refused with "already active in session X" naming the blocker.
5. **Audit log:**
   - `endless sql "SELECT kind, entity_id FROM events WHERE entity_id='XXXX' ORDER BY id"` → shows `task.status_changed` and `task.claimed` for the spawn flow.
6. **Use case 2 sanity check:**
   - `cd "$(endless worktree for-task <some_other_task>)"`, then run `claude` directly (no spawn).
   - Confirm the new Claude session is *not* auto-bound to that task. Running `endless task claim` does the full claim path normally.
