# E-1206 — Record each .endless/db-ledger/ write in git immediately

## Context

Today, `.endless/db-ledger/db-entries-<nodeHex>-NNNNNN.jsonl` is the write-ahead log for the Endless SQLite DB. Every endless CLI mutation appends one JSONL line via the Go `endless-event emit` subprocess (`internal/events/writer.go:68` `Writer.Append` → `O_APPEND` write). The file is **not committed at write time** — it accumulates as dirt on main between `endless worktree land` runs, where `worktree_cmd.AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS` picks it up and bundles all accumulated lines into a single `Endless: auto-record session activity` commit.

Per E-987's resolution, write-time auto-commit is the correct pattern. E-1208 just shipped that pattern for `verbs.jsonl`. E-1206 applies the same pattern to db-ledger entries.

Volume reality check: the current segment has 1172 lines. In the same period, the git log shows 36 auto-record commits. So today's bundling collapses ~32 events into 1 commit. Without amend, E-1206 would multiply the commit count ~30×. With amend, the count stays bounded between feature commits.

## What's actually being changed

After every successful `Writer.Append`, commit `.endless/db-ledger/<segment>.jsonl` on main. Two write sites in `cmd/endless-event/main.go:148` (pre-allocation branch) and `cmd/endless-event/main.go:202` (update branch).

Per the task description's optimization note: **amend successive ledger commits into a single rolling commit between non-ledger commits**, bounded by the "is this commit pushed?" check.

## Amend strategy (proposed for v1)

Decide between **amend** and **new commit** at each write:

```
HEAD subject starts with "Endless: record ledger entry"
  AND HEAD is not reachable from any origin/* ref
  AND the index has no staged changes outside the ledger segment path
  → git commit -o <segment-path> --amend --no-edit
otherwise
  → git add <segment-path>
    git commit -o <segment-path> -m "Endless: record ledger entry"
```

This guarantees:
- One rolling ledger commit between any two non-ledger commits.
- Never amends a pushed commit (avoids force-push concerns).
- Never bundles unrelated user-staged work into our amend (the "index clean modulo ledger" check).
- Every event becomes part of git history immediately — amend captures the post-append working-tree state.

The check "HEAD is not pushed" uses `git for-each-ref refs/remotes/origin/* --contains HEAD` — empty result means not pushed. If no `origin` exists, treat as "not pushed" (safe locally).

The check "index clean modulo ledger" uses `git diff-index --cached --name-only HEAD -- ':!.endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl'` — empty output means no other paths staged.

## Code surface

**Files to modify:**
- `cmd/endless-event/main.go:148` and `:202` — after `writer.Append(line)` succeeds, call a new helper `commitLedgerSegment(projectRoot, segmentPath)`.
- `internal/events/commit.go` (new file) — `CommitLedgerSegment(projectRoot, segmentRelPath string) error`. Implements the amend-or-new-commit decision using `exec.Command("git", ...)`. Returns error on subprocess failure.
- `internal/events/commit_test.go` (new file) — Go tests covering: first commit (new), second commit (amend), commit after non-ledger commit (new), commit when index has unrelated staged file (new, not amend), commit when HEAD is pushed to fake origin (new, not amend).

**Existing utilities to reuse:**
- `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:826` `runGitRevParse` — same `exec.Command("git", ...)` pattern; existing house style for git subprocess calls.

**No changes needed:**
- `internal/events/writer.go` — the writer stays git-agnostic. The commit step is layered on top.
- `src/endless/event_bridge.py` — Python's bridge already shells out to `endless-event`; nothing to change there.
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS` — keep db-ledger in the list for now. E-1272 (the bundled land change) is the one that drops it. Once both ship, land's auto-commit step shrinks; here we just stop *creating* dirt.

## Failure handling

Per E-1208's pattern (Mike: "fail loudly until we know what failure modes look like"):

| Failure                                | Response                                                |
|----------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------|
| Project root is not a git repo         | Fail loudly. `endless-event` exits non-zero with a clear message. Mike's projects are all under git; the soften-to-silent-skip path is filed as a follow-up only when beta testers join. |
| `git add` / `git commit` / `git amend` fails | `endless-event` exits non-zero with the git stderr. The JSONL write persists; the next event's commit may amend or new-commit to recover. |
| Concurrent writers on same node        | Cannot happen — segment files are partitioned by node ID (`db-entries-<nodeHex>-...`), so two same-node writers serialize at the OS level on the file. |
| Concurrent writers on different nodes  | Acceptable. Each writes its own segment; each commits its own path. Git's ref-update lock serializes the commits. |

Consistent with E-1208's "fail loudly" stance. Non-git-project tolerance is deferred until beta-tester needs make it concrete; we'll soften the response then.

## Performance note

Each event today costs ~1 subprocess (`endless-event emit`). After E-1206, each event adds 2–3 git subprocesses (HEAD subject check, optional pushed-check, optional index-clean check, then commit-or-amend). At ~20ms per subprocess spawn on macOS, that's ~60–80ms added per event. An `endless task update` emitting 3 events would add ~200ms.

Acceptable for v1. Optimization paths if it bites: (a) batch multiple events into one commit when one CLI invocation emits multiple, (b) move to a long-lived endless-event daemon, (c) use go-git in-process. Defer all of these.

## Verification

1. **First write commits**: From a clean main checkout, `endless task add "Audit the X system"`. Expect: `git log -1 --format=%s` shows `Endless: record ledger entry`; HEAD advanced; .endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl is clean.
2. **Second write amends**: Run another `endless task update <id> --status verify`. Expect: HEAD's commit subject is still `Endless: record ledger entry`; **HEAD's parent did not advance** (HEAD's parent is unchanged; HEAD itself was amended).
3. **Non-ledger commit breaks the chain**: Run a normal commit (e.g., touch a source file and commit). Then `endless task update <id> --status ready`. Expect: new ledger commit appears as a child of the source commit.
4. **Worktree session**: From a worktree, run any endless mutation. Expect: the commit lands on main's HEAD (not the worktree branch).
5. **Dirty main preserves user work**: Stage an unrelated file change (`git add src/foo.py`). Run an endless mutation. Expect: the staged change remains staged; HEAD's ledger commit does NOT include foo.py. The "index clean modulo ledger" gate falls back to a new commit if needed.
6. **Non-git project**: Create a fresh dir (no git init), register as endless project, run a mutation. Expect: `endless-event` exits non-zero with a clear "project root is not a git repo" message. JSONL write happens first (already on disk).
7. **Tests**: `go test ./internal/events/...` — new tests cover the amend/new-commit decision matrix.

## Resolved during planning

- **Amend in v1**: ship.
- **Commit subject**: "Endless: record ledger entry" (uses the registered verb `record`).
- **Non-git project handling**: fail loudly (same as E-1208's pattern). Soften only when beta testers materialize.
- **Pushed-check scope**: `origin/*` only. Mike pushes occasionally; the check is a safety net against silent force-push potential after a manual push.

Running `endless task release` from a shell pane next to a Claude pane should resolve via the sibling fallback (same pattern claim_item / bind_item / emit_event use). Add the same 3-layer resolution: direct → 1-sibling → refuse. On 2+ matches: refuse with ambiguity message pointing to explicit-ID form. On 0 matches: keep the existing refusal message.

No changes needed in claim_item / bind_item: their explicit `_find_sibling_claude_session` blocks still run only when the resolver returned None (0 or 2+ match cases), which is exactly when their refusal policies apply.

# E-1298 — Add `just land` recipe for "endless worktree land + refresh binaries"

## Context

After E-1206 landed, the symlinked binary `/usr/local/bin/endless-event` stayed stale because nothing rebuilt the Go binaries after the merge. The result: 5 hours of ledger writes silently bypassed the new commit step.

Root cause restated through the correct lens (per Mike's PRODUCT trigger word): this is a **dev-workflow ergonomics gap, not a product concern**. Mike has to remember two commands today — `endless worktree land E-NNNN` followed by `just build` — and the cost of forgetting is invisible regression of the running CLI against the source on disk. The fix belongs in the developer-tooling layer (Justfile), NOT in the shipped product code (`src/endless/`, `cmd/`, `internal/`).

Beta-tester users will never experience this: they install endless once, they don't rebuild it, and they don't have endless's Justfile. Putting an auto-rebuild step into `worktree_cmd.land_worktree` would bake dev workflow into product code.

## What's actually being changed

Add a new Justfile recipe `land` that wraps `endless worktree land` and follows it with `just build` on success. The recipe also derives the task ID from cwd when called without arguments, so the common case is just `just land` from inside the active worktree.

**Files to modify:**
- `Justfile` — add the `land` recipe.
- `Justfile` help block (line 4-26) — add a one-line entry under "Development" or "Git".

**No changes to:**
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` — product code stays untouched.
- `cmd/`, `internal/` — same.
- `endless` CLI verbs — same.

## Recipe design

```just
# Land a task's worktree (calls `endless worktree land`), then rebuild
# binaries so /usr/local/bin/endless-* picks up any new Go code. If no
# task ID is given, derives it from cwd via `endless worktree current`.
land task_id="":
    #!/usr/bin/env bash
    set -euo pipefail
    tid="{{ "{{task_id}}" }}"
    if [ -z "$tid" ]; then
        tid=$(endless worktree current --json 2>/dev/null \
              | jq -r '.companion.task_id // empty')
        if [ -z "$tid" ]; then
            echo "just land: not inside a task worktree and no task ID given." >&2
            echo "  Usage: just land [E-NNNN]" >&2
            exit 1
        fi
        echo "→ Derived task ID from cwd: $tid"
    fi
    endless worktree land "$tid"
    echo "→ Refreshing binaries (just build)"
    just build
```

### Key details

- `task_id=""` makes the argument optional. Just exposes empty-default args via `{{ "{{task_id}}" }}` template syntax.
- `set -euo pipefail` ensures any sub-step failure aborts the recipe with a non-zero exit. Mike sees the actual error from `endless worktree land` or `just build`.
- The cwd-based derivation uses `endless worktree current --json | jq -r '.companion.task_id // empty'`. jq is already installed (`/opt/homebrew/bin/jq`).
- When called from a worktree without a task companion (foreign worktree), or from outside any worktree, the recipe errors clearly with a usage hint.
- After a successful `endless worktree land`, the cwd's worktree is gone — but `just build` resolves recipes from the Justfile's project root, not cwd, so the build runs against main's checkout (which has just received the merge). Mike's shell may report "dir removed" if he was inside the worktree; that's expected and harmless.
- `just build` is run unconditionally. We could detect "did the just-landed commits touch Go?" but the bash for that adds complexity vs. paying ~5s of mostly-cached build time per land. Optimize later if it bites.

## Help-text addition

Line ~21 of `Justfile`'s `help` recipe, under "Git" or a new "Workflow" section:

```
just land [E-NNNN]  Land a task (derives ID from cwd if omitted), then refresh binaries
```

## Verification

1. **No-arg case**: from inside `.endless/worktrees/e-1298/`, run `just land`. Expect: derives `E-1298` from cwd, lands, prints "→ Refreshing binaries", `just build` completes successfully. `bin/endless-*` modification times advance.
2. **Explicit-arg case**: from main checkout, run `just land E-NNNN` (for some other landable task). Expect: passes through to `endless worktree land E-NNNN`, then rebuilds.
3. **Outside worktree, no arg**: from main checkout (not in a worktree), run `just land`. Expect: error message "not inside a task worktree and no task ID given" with usage hint; exit non-zero.
4. **Land failure**: if `endless worktree land` fails (e.g., dirty user-work on main), the recipe exits before the build runs. Mike sees the actual land error.
5. **Build failure post-land**: if `just build` fails after a successful land, Mike sees the build error; the land's git state is already in place.

## Out of scope

- Changes to `endless` CLI verbs or any product code under `src/endless/` or `cmd/` or `internal/`.
- A generic post-land hook mechanism (sibling of E-1086). Possibly a future task if multiple projects ever need this pattern.
- The architectural question "should Go binaries be live-editable somehow?" That's a real product-shaped concern but explicitly out of scope for this task (Mike's PRODUCT trigger word ruled it out).

## What this does NOT do

- Does not auto-trigger on the bare `endless worktree land` command. If Mike (or a future agent) runs the endless verb directly, no rebuild happens — same as today. The recipe is opt-in via the Justfile alias.
- Does not add detection / conditional logic. Unconditional `just build` after every land.

fields.text:
# E-1291 — Auto-bind session to task by cwd-in-worktree on SessionStart

## Context

After E-1274 landed, the spawn flow does the full claim (status flip + worktree creation) before launching Claude, and `SessionStart`'s auto-bind keys off `@endless_spawned_by` to record the session→task binding. Spawned sessions land in a worktree fully claimed.

For the non-spawn case — a user who manually cd's into an existing worktree and runs `claude` directly (e.g., `cd .endless/worktrees/e-NNN && claude`, `eswt E-NNN`, or any equivalent) — `handleWorktreeAdoption` claims the filesystem lock but no session→task binding is set. The user has to run `endless task claim E-NNN` to get the binding.

In Mike's standard workflow this case is uncommon (he runs `cd /path/to/project && claude` in the main checkout, brainstorms, then `endless task add` + `endless task claim`; the binding gets set via the post-bash `endless task claim` detector at `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:597`). E-1291 closes the small remaining gap for users who do go straight into a worktree — bind from the companion file's `task_id` so they don't have to also run `claim`.

## Recorded design decisions

- **Bind only, not auto-claim.** Set `sessions.active_task_id`; do not flip `tasks.status`. Status mutations should not be a side effect of a hook event the user didn't directly invoke.
- **Skip Agent-tool subagents.** `SessionStart` fires for subagents too, with their own distinct `session_id` but the parent's `cwd`. Auto-binding them would create phantom co-owners on the task. Add `AgentID` (and `AgentType`, `Source`) to `claudePayload`; gate the new block on `AgentID == ""`.
- **Don't branch on the `Source` field.** `resume`/`clear`/`compact` cases re-fire `SessionStart` but binding is idempotent (`active_task_id` ends up at the same value), so a single cwd check suffices regardless of source.
- **Don't tighten the spawn-flow path's missing subagent guard here.** Filed as separate follow-up (see below).

## Approach

Python-free change. Only touches `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` and uses existing Go helpers in `internal/monitor`.

### 1. Extend `claudePayload`

```go
type claudePayload struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    Source    string `json:"source,omitempty"`     // "startup" | "resume" | "clear" | "compact"
    AgentID   string `json:"agent_id,omitempty"`   // present for Agent-tool subagents
    AgentType string `json:"agent_type,omitempty"` // present for Agent-tool subagents
}
```

Only `AgentID` is consulted in this change; the other two are added for future use and so we don't have to roll the struct again on the first follow-up that needs them.

### 2. Add a cwd-bind block in the `SessionStart` arm

In `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go`, inside `case "SessionStart"`, after `handleWorktreeAdoption(...)` succeeds and before `handleTaskContextInjection(...)`:

```go
// Cwd-based auto-bind (E-1291): if cwd is inside an endless worktree
// and the spawn-marker path didn't already bind, set the session's
// active_task_id from the worktree companion. Skipped for Agent-tool
// subagents (they share parent cwd but represent tool use, not user
// claim intent). Bind only — does not flip task status.
if payload.AgentID == "" && tmuxSpawnedBy() == "" {
    if wtRoot, err := monitor.FindWorktreeRoot(payload.CWD, projectRoot); err == nil && wtRoot != "" {
        if companion, err := monitor.ReadWorktreeCompanion(wtRoot); err == nil && companion != nil {
            if taskID := parseCanonicalTaskID(companion.TaskID); taskID > 0 {
                monitor.BindSessionToTask(payload.SessionID, projectID, taskID)
            }
        }
    }
}
```

`parseCanonicalTaskID` is a small helper that strips the `E-` prefix and parses the integer. (If a similar helper already exists in `internal/monitor` or `internal/events`, reuse instead of adding.)

`projectRoot` is already available in the surrounding code (used by `handleWorktreeAdoption` at claude.go:1172).

### 3. No documentation update needed

The orchestration guide doesn't currently advertise the manual `cd <worktree> && claude` pattern as a flow — and per Mike, it's uncommon. This change is a quiet quality-of-life fix; no user-visible doc surface needs updating.

## Critical files

- `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` — add `Source` / `AgentID` / `AgentType` to `claudePayload` (struct around line 42-50); add the cwd-bind block in the `SessionStart` switch arm (around line 162, after `handleWorktreeAdoption`)

## Reused / not new

- `monitor.FindWorktreeRoot(cwd, projectRoot)` — `internal/monitor/worktree_lock.go:220`
- `monitor.ReadWorktreeCompanion(worktreePath)` — `internal/monitor/worktree_lock.go:53`
- `monitor.BindSessionToTask(sessionID, projectID, taskID)` — `internal/monitor/session.go` (added in E-1274)

## Verification

Run from the worktree (`.endless/worktrees/e-1291`):

1. **Build:** `just build` from the main checkout. Confirm clean `go build`.
2. **Existing tests:** `just test` (Python) + `go test ./internal/monitor/... ./cmd/endless-hook/...`.
3. **New unit tests** in `cmd/endless-hook/` covering:
   - Subagent payload (`AgentID` set) + cwd in a worktree → no bind.
   - Cwd not in any worktree (main checkout) → no bind.
   - Cwd in a worktree, no `@endless_spawned_by`, no `AgentID` → binds; verify `sessions.active_task_id` reflects the companion's task.
   - Cwd in a worktree, `@endless_spawned_by` set → spawn-flow path fires; new block is short-circuited (no double-write); session ends up bound (via spawn-flow path), task status unchanged from the cwd-bind path's perspective.
4. **Smoke test:**
   - Claim a fresh task in one tmux pane: `endless task claim E-XYZ` (creates worktree).
   - In a different terminal, `cd <main>/.endless/worktrees/e-XYZ`, then `claude`.
   - Confirm `endless sql "SELECT session_id, active_task_id FROM sessions ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 3"` shows the new session bound to E-XYZ.
   - Confirm `endless task show E-XYZ` still reports the same status it had before (no flip).
5. **Regression check:** in the standard workflow (`cd /path/to/project && claude`), confirm `SessionStart` does not bind (cwd has no companion, no auto-bind path fires). Behavior unchanged.

## Follow-up

Filed after implementation lands:

```
endless task add "Skip subagents in spawn-flow SessionStart auto-bind" --cleans-up E-1291 \
  --description "The @endless_spawned_by auto-bind block in cmd/endless-hook/claude.go's SessionStart arm doesn't currently guard against Agent-tool subagents — a subagent firing SessionStart inside a spawn-marked tmux window will bind its own session_id to the spawned task, creating a phantom co-owner. Same latent issue E-1291 addressed for the cwd path; analogous fix on the spawn-flow path. Skip when payload.AgentID != ''."
```

fields.outcome:
Shipped `endless session activity` CLI verb and "Session Activity" tmux menu item.

CLI: `endless session activity [<session-ref>] [--kinds <list>] [--json]`. Default session = current (3-layer resolver from E-1294). Accepts ES-NNN / E-NNN / plain integer. Reads `.endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl`, filters by `actor.session_id`, groups by event kind into the archetype-2 shape (Filed, Decisions, Claimed, Shipped → verify, Confirmed, Assumed, Declined, Obsoleted, Released, Dependencies, Field updates).

Tmux: prefix+e and right-click menus get "Session Activity" (hotkey `a`) opening the report in an 80%/80% display-popup paged by less.

Smoke test on dev DB (session 356) returned the expected 16 events grouped correctly:
  Filed (5): E-1287, E-1290, E-1292, E-1294, E-1299
  Claimed (3): E-1287, E-1294, E-1285
  Confirmed (1): E-1294
  Field updates: 4

327 tests pass. Verification deferred to land+install — exercise: `endless session activity` (from any pane resolvable to the current Claude session) should print the same shape; prefix+e → `a` opens it in a popup.

payload.title: Skip subagents in spawn-flow SessionStart auto-bind
payload.description: The @endless_spawned_by auto-bind block at cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:152-156 doesn't guard against Agent-tool subagents. A subagent firing SessionStart inside a spawn-marked tmux window will bind its own session_id to the spawned task, creating a phantom co-owner on the task. Same latent issue E-1291 addressed for the cwd path; analogous fix on the spawn-flow path: skip when payload.AgentID != ''.

payload.title: Audit readers of .endless/worktree.json companion task_id field and migrate to DB
payload.description: Three sources of truth currently encode 'what task does this worktree belong to': the DB sessions.active_task_id (canonical, what tmux trusts), the path convention .endless/worktrees/e-NNN/ (cheap derivation), and the .endless/worktree.json companion's task_id field (drifts when leftover). The companion's task_id can lie: a stale file in main reported E-1186 while tmux correctly showed E-1298, defeating E-1298's first fix attempt. Audit every reader of the companion's task_id (endless worktree current, _branch_for_task, etc.) and either route them through the DB session→task binding or treat the field as advisory rather than authoritative. The companion file can stay as a marker of 'endless-managed worktree' for foreign-tree classification, but its task_id must not be a source of truth.

payload.title: Add 'endless task id' and 'endless tmux task' for user-facing session task ID lookup
payload.description: endless-tmux active-id is currently undocumented plumbing for the tmux menus but its output (the DB-backed session->task binding) is exactly what users and agents need. Add 'endless task id' as the primary user-facing form (Python wrapper that shells to endless-tmux active-id or queries the DB directly). Add 'endless tmux task' as an alias for users/agents who think 'tmux' first. Both should print a single 'E-NNNN' line on success and exit non-zero on no active task. This is what 'just land' now uses internally; promoting it lets other recipes/agents rely on it the same way.

payload.title: Clear sessions.active_task_id on worktree land and terminal-status transitions
payload.description: The DB session->task binding outlives the things it points at. After 'endless worktree land E-1298' removed the worktree, and after 'endless task assume E-1298' moved the task to terminal status, the bound session's active_task_id still reads E-1298. Reproduced today: 'just land' from main correctly derived E-1298 via endless-tmux active-id, then errored 'No endless-managed worktree for E-1298'. Fix at the source: (a) worktree land's success path should UPDATE sessions SET active_task_id=NULL WHERE active_task_id=<task_id>; (b) task-status transitions to terminal states (assumed/confirmed/obsolete/declined) should do the same. Related to E-1301's broader 'companion task_id is unreliable' theme — same class of staleness, different storage location.

fields.description:
After 'endless worktree land' or a terminal-status transition (assumed/confirmed/obsolete/declined), the session->task binding in sessions.active_task_id can outlive the landed worktree. Result: 'endless-tmux active-id' (and anything else reading the binding) keeps reporting the now-stale task. Reproduced 2026-05-13: after E-1298 landed via 'just land', the session was still bound to E-1298, and the next 'just land' derived E-1298 and errored 'No endless-managed worktree for E-1298'.

Design: do NOT auto-clear (the user may want to keep the binding for follow-up work). Instead: interactive prompt with default-no-clear, plus mutually-exclusive --release / --no-release flags for non-interactive use. Apply the same pattern to terminal task-status transitions ('endless task update --status assumed --release').

A defensive check in 'just land' is being filed separately as a recipe fix, since the source design now intentionally allows stale bindings.

fields.title: Add --release/--no-release prompt to worktree land and terminal task-status transitions

payload.title: Make 'just land' defensive against stale active-id when worktree is gone
payload.description: Recipe currently derives task ID via 'endless-tmux active-id' and proceeds to land. If the binding is stale (worktree already landed but session still bound; per E-1303's design that's an intentional possibility), the land errors 'No endless-managed worktree for E-NNNN'. Add a defensive check after derivation: if the derived task has no active worktree per 'endless worktree list', skip that source and fall through to the path-regex fallback or error. Keeps the recipe robust without dictating session-binding behavior.

payload.title: Add merge=union to .gitignore for .endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl
payload.description: The DB ledger files (.endless/db-ledger/db-entries-*.jsonl) are append-only event logs but lack a merge driver in .gitattributes. Concurrent appends on main and a worktree branch produce a 'CONFLICT (content)' on rebase even when both sides only added non-overlapping lines (the worktree branch's lines often turn out to be a subset of main's, since the same events landed in both via parallel auto-record commits). E-1268 already solved this exact problem for verbs.jsonl by adding 'merge=union' in .gitattributes — apply the same pattern to db-ledger/*.jsonl. Same-line duplicates produced by union merge are absorbed at load time by the event reader's natural idempotency on (ts, kind, entity_id). Will eliminate the recurring 'rebase failed on ledger' that has bitten multiple sessions and currently requires the manual 'git checkout main -- .endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl + git rebase --skip' dance.

payload.title: Fix esp shell helper to auto-resolve sibling Claude pane when ENDLESS_SESSION_ID unset
payload.description: Per 'endless guide orchestration', 'esu, esp, and esf all auto-resolve to the sibling Claude pane in tmux when called with no argument.' But the current esp body in src/endless/cli.py's _SHELL_INIT_SNIPPET guards 'if [ -z ENDLESS_SESSION_ID ] && [ $# -eq 0 ]' and bails before the auto-resolution path runs. esu doesn't have this guard — it just calls the CLI, which handles sibling-pane resolution. esp should be symmetric: drop the early return and let _endless_run session cd --target project handle the resolution (or fall through to its own error message if no sibling Claude pane exists).

payload.title: Add 'endless session id' verb to print current session ID
payload.description: Parallel to the in-flight 'endless task id' work: add 'endless session id' that prints the active Claude session's id, resolving via ENDLESS_SESSION_ID first, then the same sibling-pane companion-file scan that 'session use' already uses. Output: bare integer (Endless session id) or UUID prefix, one per line, exits non-zero when no session can be resolved. Useful for scripts and for users who want the id without exporting or cd-ing.

payload.title: Detect already-landed branches in worktree land and message accordingly
payload.description: When 'endless worktree land E-NNN' runs after E-NNN's worktree was already removed by a prior land (common in multi-session workflows where one session lands and another tries to land), the current error is 'No endless-managed worktree for E-NNN' — which implies the work is lost. It isn't; the branch is in main. Detect this case: if a branch named task/NNN-* is fully merged into main (git branch --merged main), report 'E-NNN already landed (commit <sha>); the worktree was removed by the earlier land. No action needed.' instead of the error. Falls back to today's error message only when the branch genuinely doesn't exist anywhere.

fields.text:
# E-1275 — Record each snapshot write in git immediately

## Context

E-1275 is the third in the write-time auto-commit sequence (after E-1208 for verbs.jsonl and E-1206 for db-ledger). It applies the same pattern to plan-file snapshots: the snapshot writer at `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:888` (`snapshotPlanFile`) currently writes `<project>/.endless/plans/snapshots/<ts>-<sha8>.{md,json}` and returns; it does not commit. The files accumulate as dirt on main until `worktree land` bundles them.

Per E-987's resolution, write-time commit is the correct pattern. After E-1275 lands, the only remaining piece is E-1272 (drop `land`'s auto-commit step entirely + replace the destructive recovery hint) — all three auto-managed file types will commit at their write point.

## What's actually being changed

After `snapshotPlanFile` writes both files (the .md and the .json), commit them together on main as one commit with subject `Endless: snapshot plan`. Same amend-or-new-commit rolling pattern as E-1206 so successive snapshots between non-snapshot commits coalesce into one commit.

## Code organization

E-1206's `internal/events/commit.go` already implements the amend-or-new-commit decision for ledger segments. E-1275 needs nearly identical logic with three differences: two paths instead of one, a different commit subject, and a different "index-clean modulo X" exclude path. Refactor approach:

1. **Extract the shared logic** into a private helper `commitPaths(projectRoot string, paths []string, subject string) error` in `internal/events/commit.go`.
2. **Keep `CommitLedgerSegment`** as a thin wrapper: `return commitPaths(projectRoot, []string{segmentRel}, LedgerCommitSubject)`.
3. **Add `CommitSnapshotPair(projectRoot, mdRel, jsonRel string) error`** as a thin wrapper: `return commitPaths(projectRoot, []string{mdRel, jsonRel}, SnapshotCommitSubject)`.
4. Add constant `SnapshotCommitSubject = "Endless: snapshot plan"`.

The package name `internal/events` is slightly misleading for snapshot logic but the refactor keeps the helper colocated with `CommitLedgerSegment` and avoids a new package for two small wrappers. The right time to extract `internal/git/commit.go` is when there's a third unrelated caller; defer that.

### Amend semantics

Same as E-1206. The shared helper compares HEAD's subject to the subject being committed (not a literal string), so:
- HEAD subject `Endless: record ledger entry` + new snapshot commit → subjects differ → new commit on top.
- HEAD subject `Endless: snapshot plan` + new snapshot commit → subjects match → amend (rolling).
- HEAD subject anything else + new snapshot commit → new commit.

The `git commit -o <paths>...` works for multiple paths. When amending, `-o` stages just the named paths from the working tree; HEAD's existing tree for other paths is preserved, so prior snapshots already in HEAD remain in the amended commit while the new snapshot is added.

### Index-clean check

The existing "index clean modulo ledger dir" check needs generalizing to "index clean modulo a path glob." Pass the exclude glob as a parameter:

- `commitPaths(..., excludeGlob ".endless/db-ledger/*.jsonl")` for ledger
- `commitPaths(..., excludeGlob ".endless/plans/snapshots/*")` for snapshots

Or derive the exclude glob from the paths themselves — e.g., the directory of the first path. The latter is more concise; both work.

## Code surface

**Files to modify:**
- `internal/events/commit.go` — extract `commitPaths` private helper; add `CommitSnapshotPair` and `SnapshotCommitSubject`; rewrite `CommitLedgerSegment` as a wrapper. Same package, same file.
- `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:933` (end of `snapshotPlanFile`) — after the json write succeeds, call `events.CommitSnapshotPair(projectRoot, mdRel, jsonRel)`. Compute the relative paths from snapsDir + stem.
- `internal/events/commit_test.go` — add tests mirroring E-1206's tests but for the snapshot subject and multi-path commit. Specifically: first snapshot creates commit, second amends (rolling), ledger-commit-then-snapshot is a new commit (subjects differ), unrelated staged path prevents amend, pushed HEAD starts new commit, non-git fails loudly.

**Existing utilities to reuse:**
- `internal/events/commit.go` `runGit` / `runGitOutput` — already there.
- `internal/events/commit.go` `ensureGitRepo` — gates non-git projects (fails loudly).

## Failure handling — fail loudly (matches E-1206/E-1208)

Per Mike's stance ("fail loudly until we know what failure modes look like"), align with E-1206/E-1208. This **changes** the existing non-fatal contract for `snapshotPlanFile`:

- **Before**: `snapshotPlanFile` returned errors; the caller at `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:386–397` logged `"plan snapshot: %v"` and continued.
- **After**: `snapshotPlanFile` returns errors (file-write OR commit failures); the caller propagates the error and the hook exits non-zero.

Update the justifying comment block at `claude.go:387–396` to reflect the new policy. The "best-effort, self-healing" rationale was reasonable when snapshots were write-only; once we're also committing to git, a silent failure means the snapshot misses git history without surfacing the problem.

Scope clarification: this broadens the change beyond just the commit step (existing file-write failures will also become loud). That's a deliberate consequence of the "fail loudly" choice — gain visibility into all snapshot failure modes, soften specific paths later if telemetry shows benign noise.

## Verification

1. **First snapshot commits**: edit a plan file via Claude Write hook. Expect: `git log -1 --format=%s` shows `Endless: snapshot plan`; the two snapshot files appear in the commit's diff; `git status` clean for the snapshots dir.
2. **Second snapshot amends**: edit the plan again (different content). Expect: HEAD's commit subject is still `Endless: snapshot plan`; HEAD's parent unchanged; HEAD's diff now contains both pairs of snapshot files (rolling commit).
3. **Ledger commit between snapshots starts new commit**: run an endless mutation (triggers a ledger commit) between two plan-file writes. Expect: ledger commit on top of first snapshot; second snapshot is a new commit on top of the ledger commit (different subjects break the amend chain).
4. **Stale staged user-work prevents amend**: stage an unrelated file change before triggering a second snapshot. Expect: new commit (not amend); staged file remains staged.
5. **Pushed snapshot HEAD starts new commit**: simulate push (fake origin); subsequent snapshot starts a new commit.
6. **Non-git project fails loudly via `ensureGitRepo`**: but the caller (`snapshotPlanFile`) logs and continues per the non-fatal contract. The hook overall keeps working.
7. **Idempotent snapshots don't commit**: trigger two snapshots of the SAME content+session. The second is skipped by `existingSnapshot` early-return; no second commit.
8. **Tests**: `go test ./internal/events/...` for the commit decision matrix.

## Out of scope

- Moving `internal/events/commit.go` to `internal/git/commit.go` (or similar package). Refactor only when a third unrelated caller appears.
- Changing the snapshot failure mode from non-fatal to fail-loud. If we want fail-loud later, that's a one-line change in `snapshotPlanFile`'s caller.
- Anything in `worktree_cmd.AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS` — E-1272 owns that.

## Resolved during planning

- **Refactor scope**: extract `commitPaths` private helper now (two callers justify the abstraction).
- **Commit subject**: `Endless: snapshot plan` (shortest of the proposed forms).
- **Failure mode**: fail loudly. Change `snapshotPlanFile`'s caller to propagate the error instead of swallowing with `log.Printf`. Broadens the existing "non-fatal snapshot" contract — that's the intended outcome per Mike's "fail loudly until we know what fails" stance.
- **Exclude-glob parameter**: pass explicitly to `commitPaths`. Clear at call sites and avoids deriving from path internals.

payload.title: Route ledger auto-commits to project main checkout, not caller cwd
payload.description: Today endless-event commits the ledger on whichever git repo's HEAD the caller passes as --project-root. When called from a worktree session, the commit lands on the worktree's task branch, accumulating 'Endless: record ledger entry' commits that conflict on rebase (hit on E-1300's land 2026-05-13). Fix: event_bridge.emit_event always passes the project's main checkout (from projects table) so JSONL appends and git commits both target main; worktree branches stay ledger-free. Same routing likely applies to verbs.jsonl and snapshots; verify each. Doesn't apply when E-1281's sandbox is active — sandbox writes don't touch main at all.

fields.description: Three concerns: (a) shipping path — .gitattributes carries 'verbs.jsonl merge=union' in this repo, but for v1.0 ensure adopting projects get the entry (auto-generate on init or document). (b) Opt-out fallback for projects that cannot use custom merge drivers (corporate policy, etc.). (c) The merge problem may dissolve entirely if E-1309's routing rule (commit ledger/verbs/snapshots to main regardless of caller cwd) also applies to verbs.jsonl — worktree branches would then never carry verbs.jsonl changes, so no merge surface remains. Check E-1309's outcome before designing a verbs-specific merge handler; we may not need one.

fields.text:
# E-1272 — Replace worktree land's destructive recovery hint; drop auto-commit step

## Context

Per E-987's resolution, after E-1206 / E-1208 / E-1275 all commit endless-managed files at write time, worktree land's batch auto-commit step at `worktree_cmd.py:702-730` has nothing left to do — any dirt in those files at land time indicates a routing/write-time bug (or stale-state edge case), not normal accumulation. The destructive recovery hint at `:744-759` ("git checkout main -- ...") is dangerous if a real conflict ever occurs (it silently overwrites legitimate changes). Both should change.

The just-filed E-1309 (route ledger commits to main regardless of caller cwd) plus the already-landed write-time commits make this cleanup safe: by the time someone tries to land, there shouldn't be uncommitted endless-managed dirt on main; if there is, it's an invariant violation worth surfacing, not silently bundling.

## Changes

### 1. Drop the batch auto-commit step (worktree_cmd.py:702-730)

Replace the `_git_status_partition` + auto-commit logic with a single refusal check: if `git status --porcelain` from main shows ANY dirty paths — auto-managed OR otherwise — refuse with a message that names the dirty paths, points at the write-time-commit invariant, and instructs the user to investigate.

`AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS`, `_is_auto_commit_path`, and `_git_status_partition` themselves stay in place — they're still used by tests and may be useful for diagnostic logging. Only the *behavior* changes: no more bundling-and-committing.

### 2. Replace the destructive recovery hint (worktree_cmd.py:744-759)

Replace the `git checkout main -- <globs>` recovery hint with a non-destructive investigation message: name the conflicted paths from the rebase error, point at the E-972 routing invariant, and instruct the user to investigate (`git log -p`, `git blame`) rather than overwrite. Mention E-1309 as the underlying fix in flight.

## Critical files

- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:702-730` — drop auto-commit step
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:744-759` — replace recovery hint

## Verification

1. `just test` — existing suite passes. `tests/test_worktree_land_dedup.py`'s `AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS` / `_is_auto_commit_path` tests should still pass since the constants/helpers remain.
2. Smoke: in a worktree with no dirt on main, `endless worktree land E-NNN` succeeds (unchanged).
3. Smoke: synthesize uncommitted `.endless/verbs.jsonl` dirt on main, attempt land — should refuse with the new message naming the dirty path.

## Out of scope

- Removing `AUTO_COMMIT_GLOBS` constant / dependents — defer.
- Refactoring `_dedup_worktree_verbs_against_main` — separate concern.
- E-1309's actual implementation — separate task.

# E-1301 — Audit and migrate readers of `.endless/worktree.json` task_id field

## Context

E-1298 surfaced the "man with two watches" problem: a stale `.endless/worktree.json` left in main from an older session reported `E-1186` while tmux's DB-backed lookup correctly showed `E-1298`. The recipe trusted the companion file and tried to land the wrong task. The fix in E-1298 stopped reading the companion locally, but other code paths still trust the field. This task closes the systemic loop.

Per the task description, the design intent (matches Mike's framing):

> The companion file can stay as a marker of 'endless-managed worktree' for foreign-tree classification, but its `task_id` must not be a source of truth.

The right alternative source of truth is the **path convention** (`<project>/.endless/worktrees/e-NNN[/-slug]?`). The directory name IS the canonical task ID, established by `endless task claim` and enforced by the convention. Path-extraction is:
- Cheap (string regex; no file read, no DB query)
- Stale-proof (the dir name doesn't drift; if the path is renamed by hand, the worktree is broken at a more fundamental level)
- Self-documenting (the path matches what the user sees in `cd` and `ls`)

`internal/monitor/companion.go:WorktreePathForTask` already uses path convention going the other direction (task → path); E-1301 makes the reverse direction (path → task) consistent.

## Inventory of readers

Eight call-sites, six Python + two Go. All but two are lookups; the other two are display.

| # | File:line | Function | Use | Migration |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:238` | `_enriched_list` | Display task in `worktree list` | path-extraction |
| 2 | `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:276–277` | `current_worktree` | Display task in `worktree current` | path-extraction |
| 3 | `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:336` | `for_task` | Find worktree row by task_id | path-extraction (or use `WorktreePathForTask` semantics) |
| 4 | `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:516–520` | `_branch_for_task` | Find worktree row by task_id (used in `land`) | path-extraction |
| 5 | `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py:624` | `create_task_worktree` | Reject double-claim by checking existing companion | path-extraction |
| 6 | `src/endless/task_cmd.py:_worktree_for_task` | Plan-file routing ownership | path-extraction |
| 7 | `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:1102` | `resolveCwdTaskID` | PostToolUse task auto-binding | path-extraction |
| 8 | `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go:1372–1383` | Task-mismatch tool-use block message | path-extraction |

## Design

**Step 1**: introduce two small helpers and use them everywhere.

- **Python**: `_task_id_from_worktree_path(p: Path) -> str | None` in `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py`. Regex-extracts `E-NNN` from a path matching `.../\.endless/worktrees/e-(\d+)(-[a-z0-9-]*)?(/|$)`. Returns the canonical `E-NNN` or `None` if no match. Pure function, no I/O.
- **Go**: `TaskIDFromWorktreePath(path string) string` in `internal/monitor/worktree_lock.go` (sits next to `FindWorktreeRoot`). Same semantics, returns `""` on no match.

**Step 2**: replace each `comp.task_id` / `comp.TaskID` read with the helper.

For the lookups (#3, #4, #5, #6, #7), the typical shape changes from:

```python
# before
target = next(
    (r for r in rows
     if r["companion"] and r["companion"].get("task_id") == canonical),
    None,
)
# after
target = next(
    (r for r in rows
     if _task_id_from_worktree_path(Path(r["path"])) == canonical),
    None,
)
```

For display readers (#1, #2), substitute the path-derived value. Display matches the existing column expectations because path-extraction returns the same `E-NNN` string when the convention holds.

For Go #7 (`resolveCwdTaskID`):

```go
// before
comp, err := monitor.ReadWorktreeCompanion(wtRoot)
if err != nil || comp == nil || comp.TaskID == "" {
    return 0
}
taskID, err := parseEndlessTaskID(comp.TaskID)
// after
tid := monitor.TaskIDFromWorktreePath(wtRoot)
if tid == "" {
    return 0
}
taskID, err := parseEndlessTaskID(tid)
```

`FindWorktreeRoot` (which walks up looking for `.endless/worktree.json`) still uses the companion **as a marker of "endless-managed"**, but no longer reads its task_id field. Consistent with Mike's "marker, not source of truth."

**Step 3**: stop writing `task_id` to the companion file on new worktree creation (`worktree_cmd.create_task_worktree:650`). The struct field stays (with `omitempty`) for backward-compat reads — old companion files keep working — but the staleness surface shrinks: new worktrees never carry a writable copy that could lie.

**Step 4**: when reading any code path that still encounters a populated `task_id` field (legacy companion files), compare it to the path-extracted task_id. On disagreement, emit a stderr warning so the staleness is visible. The path-extracted value wins; the warning is informational, not blocking. This catches legacy-companion drift without re-introducing trust in the field.

## Scope clarification: cwd-based vs worktree-path-based readers

Two classes of readers:

**Worktree-path-based (6 of 8 sites)** — input is an already-resolved worktree path; cwd is irrelevant.
- `_enriched_list` iterates `git worktree list` and reads each row's path.
- `for_task`, `_branch_for_task`, `_worktree_for_task`, `create_task_worktree` start from a task ID and check the deterministic path `.endless/worktrees/e-NNN/`.
- Go's task-mismatch block (claude.go:1372) operates on an already-resolved `worktreePath`.

For these, path-extraction is correct regardless of session cwd.

**cwd-based (2 of 8 sites)** — input is cwd; walks up.
- `current_worktree`: from a worktree, extracts E-NNN; from main, no extraction (correctly reports "main, no task").
- `resolveCwdTaskID` (Go hook): from a worktree, returns E-NNN; from main, returns 0 — no auto-bind attempted. This is correct for the "user starts in main, claim creates a worktree, session stays in main" case: the explicit `task claim` already wrote the DB session→task binding, so the hook does not need to also auto-bind. The DB binding (`sessions.active_task_id`) remains the canonical source for "what task is this session on" regardless of cwd; `endless-tmux active-id` reads it correctly from anywhere.

The plan does not regress the main-cwd-but-task-bound scenario. Path-extraction answers "what task is THIS PATH for"; the DB binding answers "what task is THIS SESSION on". Different questions, different sources.

## What this does NOT touch

- `internal/monitor/companion.go:WorktreePathForTask` — already path-based; no change.
- The DB session→task binding (`sessions.active_task_id`) — that's a separate canonical source for "which task is this session working on" and remains untouched.
- The companion's `branch`, `base_branch`, `created_at`, `kind` fields — out of scope; this task audits only `task_id`.
- E-1303 (release-prompt on land/terminal) — overlapping in spirit (stale-state cleanup) but different mechanism. Stays independent.

## Code surface

**Files to modify:**
- `src/endless/worktree_cmd.py` — add `_task_id_from_worktree_path` helper; update 5 call-sites (#1–#5).
- `src/endless/task_cmd.py` — update `_worktree_for_task` (#6); import the helper.
- `internal/monitor/worktree_lock.go` — add `TaskIDFromWorktreePath` function.
- `cmd/endless-hook/claude.go` — update `resolveCwdTaskID` (#7) and the task-mismatch block (#8).

**Tests to add or update:**
- `tests/test_worktree_create.py` (or similar) — unit tests for `_task_id_from_worktree_path` (matches, no-match, with-slug, in-subdir, in-main-checkout).
- `internal/monitor/worktree_lock_test.go` — unit tests for `TaskIDFromWorktreePath`.
- Existing tests for `_branch_for_task`, `for_task`, `current_worktree`, etc. — verify behavior unchanged when worktrees follow the convention.
- New regression test: a worktree with a deliberately-wrong companion `task_id` (e.g., simulating the E-1186 stale case) — verify readers ignore the companion's lie and use the path.

## Verification

1. **Stale-companion immunity (regression)**: create a worktree at `.endless/worktrees/e-999/`, hand-edit its companion's `task_id` to `E-12345`, run `endless worktree current` from inside the worktree. Expect: `Task: E-999` (path wins; companion's lie ignored).
2. **Main checkout reports no task**: with no `.endless/worktree.json` in main (clean state) OR with a leftover stale one, `endless worktree current` from main reports `Task:` empty. The lie-prone case is fundamentally fixed by path-extraction.
3. **All Go tests pass**: `go test ./...` green.
4. **All Python tests pass**: `just test` green.
5. **Smoke**: `endless worktree list` shows correct tasks for live worktrees; `endless worktree for-task E-NNNN` resolves the right path.

## Out of scope

- Removing the `task_id` field from the companion file. Keeping it for human readability is harmless once nothing reads it as source of truth.
- A migration script to clean up existing stale companion files. The fix is read-side; stale files become irrelevant after migration.
- E-1303 / E-1304 (release prompt, recipe defensive check). Tracked separately.

## Resolved during planning

- **Helper location (Python)**: in `worktree_cmd.py`, imported from `task_cmd.py`. Adds a forward `task_cmd → worktree_cmd` import; no cycle.
- **Disagreement warning**: emit a stderr warning when the legacy companion's `task_id` disagrees with path-extraction. Path-extraction wins; warning is informational, helps spot stale companion files.
- **Stop writing `task_id` to new companion files**: shrink the staleness surface. Old companions keep their field (we don't migrate disk state); new writes omit it. Struct field stays with `omitempty` so reads of legacy files still parse.

payload.title: Reduce DefaultMaxEventsPerSegment so ledger files stay IDE-loadable
payload.description: internal/events/writer.go's DefaultMaxEventsPerSegment is 10000. Current file at 1237 lines is already 1.4MB (~1.1KB/event), so 10000 would mean ~11MB segments. JetBrains IDEs and many editors struggle past 1-2MB, and users may occasionally need to open the ledger by hand (debugging, forensic check). Lower to 500 (~550KB max segment size). Verify no caller assumes the higher number; rotation logic at writer.go:74-78 already handles arbitrary thresholds.

fields.title: Migrate readers of .endless/worktree.json companion task_id to path convention

payload.title: Audit orphaned worktree dirs (e-1031, e-1036, e-1055) before deleting
payload.description: Three directories exist under .endless/worktrees/ but git doesn't recognize them as worktrees — their .git files point to stale metadata at .git/worktrees/task-1031-session-focus etc. (paths that no longer exist). They're invisible to endless worktree list. Likely leftovers from pre-complete worktree functionality. For each: compare its files against main to identify what task each was working on and whether the work landed; if landed, safe to delete; if not landed, preserve the work somehow (cherry-pick into a new worktree?) before deletion. Currently their .endless/db-ledger/ dirs contain main's current ledger split (copied during the E-1310 hygiene sync; harmless filler) but the rest of each directory's working tree still reflects its pre-orphan state.
